records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
18865
What was the primary purpose for establishing the Panji Prabandh 
in the Mithila region?
To maintain caste purity among Brahmins, Kayasthas, and 
Kshatriyas by tracking lineages.
18866
In the context of Mithila genealogy, what does the term 'Udegh' 
(à¤‰à¤¿à¥‡à¤¢à¤¼) refer to?
An introduction to 32 ancestors covering seven generations to 
determine marriage eligibility.
18867
According to the Panji system, what are the three main hierarchical 
divisions of Mithila Brahmins?
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddh.
18868
What is the status of those who were not formally recorded in the 
Panji registers?
They are referred to as 'Jaywar' (à¤œà¤ à¤¬à¤¾à¤¿).
18869
How many generations must a Shrotriya Brahmin typically avoid in 
the maternal lineage for marriage?
Five generations.
18870
How many generations must a Shrotriya Brahmin avoid in the 
paternal lineage for marriage?
Seven generations.
18871
How many Gotras are traditionally recognised in the Mithila 
Brahmin community?
Twenty (20) Gotras.
18872
What is the total number of 'Mools' (original lineages) identified in 
the Panji system?
One hundred and sixty-seven (167) Mools.
18873
The main 35 Mools are further categorised into which three groups? Ayanth Shrestha (First Class), Second Class, and Madhyam Mool.
18874
Which two major Vedic branches (Shakhas) do Mithila Brahmins 
primarily follow?
Yajurveda (Madhyandina Shakha) and Samaveda (Kauthuma 
Shakha).
18875
Which Gotras belong to the 'Panch-pravara' (five ancestors) 
category?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharadwaja.
18876
Under which king was the Panji Prabandh formally organised in 
Mithila?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva of the Karnata dynasty.
18877
In what year (Shaka and AD) was the Panji Prabandh established 
under Maharaja Harisimhadeva?
Shaka 1248, which corresponds to 1326 AD.
18878
Who was the scholar appointed to manage the Panji records for 
Mithila Brahmins?
Pandit Gunakar Jha.
18879
Who was appointed to oversee the Panji records for the Karna 
Kayasthas?
Shankardatt.
18880
Who was appointed to manage the Panji records for the Kshatriyas? Vijaydatt.
18881
What is the 'Siddhant Patra' (or Ashvajana Patra) in the Mithila 
marriage tradition?
A formal certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that the bride 
and groom are not prohibited relatives.
18882
The specific ancestor from whom a person is counted as the sixth 
descendant in the Panji system is known as _____.
Chhadwitt (à¤›à¤¦à¥ à¤µà¤ )
18883
How many types of 'Chhadwitt' relationships are checked for a 
bride before marriage eligibility is determined?
Sixteen (16) types.
18884
What is the rule regarding marriage between the Vatsa and Savarna 
Gotras?
They cannot intermarry because they share the same ancestors.
18885
Mithila Brahmins who follow the Samaveda are referred to as 
_____.
Chhandog (à¤›à¤¨à¥ à¤¦à¥‹à¤°à¥ )
18886
Mithila Brahmins who follow the Yajurveda are referred to as 
_____.
Vajasaneyi (à¤°à¥ à¤¾à¤œà¤¸à¤¿à¥‡à¤¯ à¥€)
18887
How does the 'Chhandog' marriage ritual differ from the 
'Vajasaneyi' regarding timing?
Chhandog rituals involve a specific evening segment to view the 
star Arundhati, which is not mandatory for Vajasaneyi.
18888
In the Panji system, the group 'Panch Gauda' includes Brahmins 
from Utkal, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Saraswat, and _____.
Maithil
18889
What is the geographic divider between the 'Panch Gauda' and 
'Panch Dravida' Brahmin groups?
The Vindhyachal Mountains.
18890
Concept: Sakulya
Individuals belonging to the same Gotra or lineage, making them 
ineligible for marriage with each other.
18891
Which philosopher mentioned 'Samuha Lekhya' (family records) in 
his work 'Tantra Vartika' during the 7th century?
Kumarila Bhatta.
18892
How many 'Laukik' or 'Chaupala' categories exist for Shrotriya 
Brahmins?
Eight (8).
18893
What is the 'Pachhwari Par' (à¤ªà¤›à¤¬à¤¾à¤°à¤¿à¤ªà¤¾à¤¿)?
A general term used for Brahmin groups other than the Shrotriyas 
(who are called Pubari Par).
18894
Process: Preservation of Panji Books
Books must be protected from water, oil, and loose binding; they 
are traditionally dried in the sun when it enters the Leo (Simha) 
constellation.
18895
Which language/script is traditionally used to write Panji records?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
18896
Who was the 'Biji Purush' of the Mandar Mool, a famous lineage in 
Mithila?
Mahamahopadhyaya Trivenibhatta.
18897
Which famous poet's genealogy is recorded across seven 
generations in the Panji books?
Vidyapati Thakur.
18898
What does the Panji record indicate about the ancestors of 
Chaitanya Mahaprabhu?
His ancestor, Ramapati Upadhyay, was from the Katih-Tilauli Mool 
of Mithila.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
18899
The system of 'Sakha Panji' (branch registers) was introduced 
during the reign of which later Maharaja?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
18900
What is the 'Tri-pravara' definition in Mithila genealogy?
A lineage tracing back to three specific Vedic sage ancestors.
18901
Which Gotra is associated with the ancestors Shandilya, Asit, and 
Deval?
Shandilya Gotra.
18902
Which Gotra is associated with the ancestors Parashara, Shakti, and 
Vasishtha?
Parashara Gotra.
18903
In the Karna Kayastha community, which is the only recognised 
Gotra?
Kashyapa Gotra.
18904
How does the Panji define 'Sapinda' for the paternal side?
Seven generations of common ancestry.
18905
According to the excerpts, what was the major consequence of 
Pandit Harinath Upadhyay marrying his cousin in the 13th century?
It led to the realisation that oral memory was insufficient and the 
formal Panji Prabandh was necessary.
18906
What is the purpose of 'Adhikar Mala'?
A list of potential grooms prepared for a bride 5â€“10 years before 
marriage, verified by a Panjikar.
18907
The term used for a Brahmin who is 'descended from the records' is 
_____.
Panjibaddh (à¤ªà¤žà¥ à¤œà¥€à¤¬à¤¦à¥ à¤§)
18908
In modern terms, the text likens the ancient Panji system to _____ 
mapping.
Genome
18909
Under what condition is a marriage permitted if a 'Chhadwitt' 
relationship is found?
Marriage is permitted if the relationship is found only on the 
maternal side and satisfies the 5-generation rule.
18910
What was the 'Tirhuta' script's relationship to the Panji system?
The Panji system is considered the 'soul' or 'life-force' that kept the 
Tirhuta script alive.
18911
Which Mool is noted for having branches in 74 different locations 
in Mithila?
Mandir Mool (Kashyapa Gotra).
18912
What rule exists for the 'Nirmaan' (mother's brother's) lineage?
The groom cannot marry the daughter of his maternal uncle.
18913
In the context of the Panji, what does 'Laukik' status signify for the 
future of a family line?
It can be 'Adhogami' (declining) if marriages are made into lower-
status lineages.
18914
What is the primary purpose of the 'Panji Prabandh' in Mithila 
culture?
To maintain genealogical records for preserving caste purity and 
determining marriage eligibility.
18915
Which historical ruler is credited with formalising the Panji system 
in Mithila?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
18916
Term: Uteh
Definition: A genealogical introduction covering seven generations 
and involving the names of 32 ancestral families.
18917
According to the Panji system, how many generations must a 
'Srotriya' Brahmin avoid on the paternal side for marriage?
Seven generations.
18918
According to the Panji system, how many generations must a 
'Srotriya' Brahmin avoid on the maternal side for marriage?
Five generations.
18919
How many generations must a 'Panjibaddh' Brahmin avoid on the 
paternal side for marriage?
Six generations.
18920
Out of the 167 'Mool Grams', how many were designated as 
primary or 'Mukhya'?
Thirty-four.
18921
What is a 'Beeji Purush' in the context of the Panji system?
The earliest known progenitor or root ancestor of a specific lineage 
(Mool).
18922
Concept: Tri-pravara
A lineage whose ancestors are credited with composing three 
specific 'Suktas' (hymns) in the Rigveda.
18923
Concept: Panch-pravara
A lineage whose ancestors are credited with composing five 
specific 'Suktas' (hymns) in the Rigveda.
18924
Which two major Vedas do Maithil Brahmins primarily follow?
Yajurveda and Samaveda.
18925
The branch of Yajurveda followed by Maithil Brahmins is known 
as _____.
Madhyandini (or Vajasaneyi).
18926
The branch of Samaveda followed by Maithil Brahmins is known 
as _____.
Kauthuma (or Chhandoga).
18927
What is the geographic dividing line between 'Pancha-Gauda' and 
'Pancha-Dravida' Brahmins?
The Vindhyachal Mountains.
18928
Name the five branches of the 'Pancha-Gauda' Brahmins.
Utkala, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, and Saraswat.
18929
The document issued by a Panjikar certifying that a couple are not 
'Svajana' (forbidden kin) is called the _____.
Siddhant Patra.
18930
Who was the first Panjikar appointed specifically for Maithil 
Brahmins?
Gunakar Jha.
18931
Who was the first Panjikar appointed for the 'Karna Kayasthas'?
Shankardatta.
18932
Who was the first Panjikar appointed for the 'Kshatriyas' in Mithila? Vijaydatta.
18933
Why is marriage forbidden between individuals of the 'Vatsa' and 
'Savarna' Gotras?
They are considered to share common ancestors, making them 
genealogically identical for marriage purposes.
18934
Which 7th-century scholar mentioned 'Samuha Lekhya' (lineage 
records) in his work 'Tantra Vartika'?
Kumarila Bhatta.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
18935
What is recorded in a 'Dushan Panji'?
Lineage flaws, historical errors, or negative traits associated with 
specific ancestors.
18936
What is the 'Chhadwith' in Panji calculations?
A set of sixteen specific genealogical points or ancestors checked to 
ensure no forbidden biological relation.
18937
Under which ruler did the 'Sakha Panji' (branch records) and 
stricter social divisions like 'Srotriya' begin?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
18938
In the Panji system, what does the term 'Sapinda' refer to?
Biological relations or kin sharing the same body particles, within 
which marriage is forbidden.
18939
How many specific ancestors are listed on the maternal side in a 
Panji 'Uteh' calculation?
Sixteen.
18940
What are the four main levels of marriage eligibility introduced in 
the later Panji system?
Srotriya, Yogya, Panjibaddh, and Jaibar.
18941
According to the Smritis of Manu and Yajnavalkya, which four 
biological lines must be avoided for a valid marriage?
Father's Gotra, Mother's Sapinda, Father's Sapinda, and specific 
maternal/paternal maternal lines.
18942
The Maithil Brahmin population is divided into how many main 
categories of 'Pravara'?
Two: Tri-pravara and Panch-pravara.
18943
What script is traditionally used to write and maintain Panji records? Tirhuta (or Mithilakshar).
18944
Which specific ancestor in the Sandilya Gotra lived in 'Mandar' and 
is considered a 'Beeji'?
Trivikram Bhatta.
18945
What is the 'Sakun' or 'Kshana' recorded in Panji documents?
A genealogical flaw or stain in the lineage that might affect 
marriage status.
18946
How many 'Srotriya Panjis' are currently recognised?
Thirty-two.
18947
How many 'Yogya' and 'Panjibaddh' Panjis are recognised?
One hundred and fifty-three.
18948
What is the significance of the Samavedic 'Uttara Vivaha'?
It is a marriage ritual performed after sunset after observing the star 
Arundhati.
18949
Term: Beeji
Definition: The root ancestor from whom a specific genealogical 
line (Mool) branches out.
18950
Which Maithil ruler is associated with the expansion of 'Sakha 
Panji' in the 18th century?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
18951
What defines a 'Svajana' marriage in the Panji system?
An illegal or forbidden marriage within prohibited genealogical 
degrees, often resulting in social exclusion.
18952
In the Panji records, which month is recommended for drying and 
maintaining the manuscripts?
Bhadrapada (when the Sun is in Leo/Simha), as the heat is intense 
enough to preserve the palm leaves.
18953
The title 'Panji Shastra Martand' was awarded to which famous 
Panjikar?
Moda Nand Jha.
18954
What is the 'Udhravadhi' division in the Panji system?
The classification of Brahmins based on their Vedic branch 
(Samaveda or Yajurveda).
18955
Which Gotra is exclusively associated with the 'Karna Kayasthas' of 
Mithila?
Kashyap.
18956
What is the primary role of the 'Siddhant' process?
To verify that the bride and groom are not related within five 
generations of the mother or six/seven of the father.
18957
Name the three Gotras that belong to the 'Panch-pravara' category.
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bhardwaj.
18958
Name the progenitor of the 'Sakadi' lineage in the Panji system.
Haridatta.
18959
The progenitor of the 'Sarisab' lineage is _____.
Ratnapani.
18960
Which ancestor is the 'Beeji' of the 'Oini' Mool?
Udyahdev.
18961
What script did the Panji system help preserve as its primary 
'lifeblood'?
Tirhuta script.
18962
According to Panji rules, why is marriage with a maternal uncle's 
daughter forbidden?
It violates the rule against marrying a descendant of the mother's 
brother (Matrimandhu).
18963
Which document lists the 32 maternal and paternal lines used for 
marriage verification?
The Uteh.
18964
Who was the 'Mahamaho-padhyaya' and composer of the 
'Purushottam' Sakha Panji?
Purushottam Jha.
18965
The classification of Brahmins based on their geographic location 
south of the Vindhyas is _____.
Pancha-Dravida.
18966
Who is credited with formally establishing the Panji Prabandha 
(genealogical records) system in Mithila?
Raja Harisimhadeva of the Karnata dynasty.
18967
In what year of the Saka era was the Panji system officially 
organized?
Saka $1248$ (approximately $1326$ AD).
18968
What is the primary purpose of the 'Uderh' in the Panji system?
To provide an introduction to seven generations of ancestors to 
ensure lineage purity.
18969
How many ancestors' introductions are required to fulfill the 
complete identification of the 'thirty-two lineages'?
Thirty-two.
18970
According to the Panji system, how many generations must be 
avoided on the father's side for a marriage to be valid?
Seven generations (later modified to six in some practices).

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
18971
According to the Panji system, how many generations must be 
avoided on the mother's side for a marriage to be valid?
Five generations.
18972
What are the three hierarchical categories of Maithil Brahmins 
created by the Panji system?
Srotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddha.
18973
What term was used to describe Brahmins who were not recorded 
in the Panji registers?
Jaibar (or Jayavaar).
18974
How many distinct Gotras are recognized within the Maithil 
Brahmin community?
Twenty.
18975
List four of the seven most common and well-populated Gotras 
among Maithil Brahmins.
Sandilya, Vatsa, Kasyapa, and Parashara (also Savarna, 
Bharadwaja, and Katyayana).
18976
What is the difference between a 'Mula' and a 'Mulagram' in Maithil 
genealogy?
Mula is the original ancestral root/village; Mulagram is the village 
where descendants later settled.
18977
Define 'Biji Purush' within the context of Maithil genealogy.
The earliest known progenitor or 'seed ancestor' from whom a 
specific lineage is traced.
18978
What is a 'Siddhanta' (or Ashvajana-patra)?
A certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that the bride and 
groom are not related within prohibited degrees.
18979
Who was appointed as the first Panjikar for the Maithil Brahmin 
community?
Gunakar Jha of the Pandua Mula.
18980
Who was the first registrar (Panjikar) appointed for the Kshatriya 
community in Mithila?
Vijayadatta.
18981
What is the 'Tri-pravara' family designation?
Gotras whose ancestors composed three specific Suktas of the 
Rigveda.
18982
What is the 'Pancha-pravara' family designation?
Gotras whose ancestors composed five specific Suktas of the 
Rigveda.
18983
Which three Gotras are specifically identified as 'Pancha-pravara'?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharva.
18984
Into which two broad geographic groups are Indian Brahmins 
divided?
Pancha-Gauda and Pancha-Dravida.
18985
The _____ mountains serve as the geographic boundary between 
the Pancha-Gauda and Pancha-Dravida Brahmins.
Vindhya.
18986
To which of the five Gauda divisions do the Maithil Brahmins 
belong?
Pancha-Gauda.
18987
What are the two primary Vedas followed by Maithil Brahmins?
Yajurveda and Samaveda.
18988
Followers of the Madhyandina Sakha of the Yajurveda are known 
as _____.
Vajasaneyi.
18989
Followers of the Kauthuma Sakha of the Samaveda are known as 
_____.
Chandoga.
18990
In Chandoga (Samaveda) tradition, marriage is performed in two 
parts, with the second part occurring after the sighting of which 
star?
Arundhati (the morning/evening star).
18991
Which 7th-century scholar mentioned 'Samuha Lekhya' (lineage 
records) in his work 'Tantra Varttika'?
Kumarila Bhatta.
18992
Under whose reign was the 'Sakha Panji' (branch records) 
introduced, leading to further social stratification?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
18993
How many 'Mulas' (ancestral roots) were initially identified for 
Maithil Brahmins?
One hundred and sixty-seven ($167$).
18994
What does a 'Dushana Panji' record?
Lineage flaws, deviations from social norms, or defects in 
genealogical purity.
18995
In the Karna Kayastha community, how many Gotras are 
traditionally used in the Panji system?
Only one: Kasyapa.
18996
The 'Gotra Panji' contains a record of all Gotras and their _____.
Ancient ancestral roots (Prachin Mula).
18997
What does the 'Paat Panji' primarily track?
Descendants (Apatya) and their specific village locations.
18998
What is the 'Chadhvith' calculation?
A process of determining sixteen specific ancestral relationships to 
verify marriage eligibility.
18999
According to the preservation rules, during which astrological 
period should Panji books be dried in the sun?
When the Sun enters the Leo (Simha) sign ($16$ August to $15$ 
September).
19000
Which Maithil scholar's interpretation of 'Sapinda' rules became the 
standard for the community's marriage laws?
Mahamaho-padhyaya Mahesh Thakur.
19001
The total number of Srotriya Panji books is _____.
Thirty-two ($32$).
19002
The total number of Panji books for the Yogya and Panjibaddha 
categories is _____.
One hundred and fifty-three ($153$).
19003
What is the 'Ayanti Shrestha' category in the Mula system?
The first or most elite category of ancestral roots, comprising 
thirteen Mulas.
19004
According to the text, what occurs if a person marries a 'Swajana' 
(a prohibited relative)?
The offspring are considered 'Chandala' and the person is expelled 
from the Aryan social order.
19005
How does the 'Vajasaneyi' (Yajurveda) marriage timing differ from 
the 'Chandoga' (Samaveda) tradition?
Vajasaneyi marriages can occur at any time (day or night) without a 
specific division into two parts.
19006
In the context of Panji preservation, what does 'Jalat Raksha, Tailat 
Raksha' mean?
Protect [the book] from water and protect it from oil.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
19007
What is the significance of the year $2000$ AD mentioned in the 
text regarding Panji records?
The project to transcribe and digitize Tirhuta Panji records into 
Devanagari began.
19008
The Madhyam Mula category consists of how many specific roots?
Eight.
19009
The root 'Sarisab' belongs to which Gotra?
Sandilya.
19010
What genealogical role does the 'Panjikar' play in Maithil society?
They act as authorized registrars who maintain lineage records and 
certify marriage eligibility.
19011
What is 'Laukika' in the context of the Srotriya category?
Specific lineage distinctions or sub-classifications used in Srotriya 
genealogical records.
19012
How are the ancestors of a Gotra defined in the text?
As the ancient sages (Rishis) who are the original progenitors of the 
entire community sharing that Gotra.
19013
Which text is cited as the source for the definition of the ten 
divisions of Brahmins (Pancha-Gauda and Pancha-Dravida)?
The Puranas.
19014
In what year was the Maithil Panji Prabandh (genealogical records) 
formally established?
1326 AD (Saka 1248).
19015
Which Karnat dynasty king is credited with the initiation of the 
Panji Prabandh system?
King Harisimhadeva.
19016
What was the primary objective for creating the Panji Prabandh 
records?
To ensure caste purity and regulate marriage eligibility.
19017
Term: Udedh
Definition: The introduction of 32 ancestral lineages (Mulas) 
required to determine marriage eligibility.
19018
In the Maithil Brahmin community, how many distinct Gotras are 
identified?
20 Gotras.
19019
According to the Panji system, how many maternal generations 
must be avoided for a valid marriage?
Five generations.
19020
According to the Panji system, how many paternal generations must 
be avoided for a valid marriage?
Seven generations (later revised to six in some practices).
19021
The earliest known ancestor of a specific lineage in the Panji 
records is called the _____.
Beeji Purush
19022
What term refers to the original ancestral village of a family in the 
Panji system?
Mula Gram
19023
Which group of Brahmins is designated as 'Shrotriya' within the 
Panji hierarchy?
The highest rank, consisting of those with the most rigorous 
genealogical and conduct standards.
19024
What are the four hierarchical categories of Maithil Brahmins 
defined after the Panji Prabandh?
Shrotriya, Yogya, Panjibaddah, and Jaywal.
19025
Who was appointed as the first Panjikar (genealogist) for the 
Maithil Brahmin community?
Gunakar Jha.
19026
Who was the first Panjikar appointed for the Kshatriya community 
in Mithila?
Vijayadatta.
19027
How many 'Mulas' (ancestral roots) were originally identified for 
the Maithil Brahmins?
167 Mulas.
19028
The 'Panch Gauda' division of Northern Indian Brahmins includes 
Utkal, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Saraswat, and _____.
Maithil
19029
Maithil Brahmins following the Sama Veda are traditionally 
referred to as _____.
Chandogya
19030
Maithil Brahmins following the Shukla Yajur Veda are traditionally 
referred to as _____.
Vajasaneyi
19031
What is the key difference in marriage rituals between Chandogya 
and Vajasaneyi Brahmins regarding timing?
Chandogya marriages are split into two parts (Purva and Uttara), 
with the latter occurring after sunset.
19032
Which unique Gotra is the only one found among the Karna 
Kayasthas of Mithila?
Kashyap Gotra.
19033
What does the term 'Chadvith' refer to in the context of marriage 
eligibility?
The 16 specific ancestral points (prohibited degrees) that must be 
checked for both bride and groom.
19034
In the Panji system, a person whose name is entered into the formal 
genealogical records is called _____.
Panjibaddah
19035
Which classification of Pravara refers to lineages identifying three 
ancestral sages?
Tri-pravara.
19036
Which classification of Pravara refers to lineages identifying five 
ancestral sages?
Panch-pravara.
19037
Identify the three Gotras that fall under the 'Panch-pravara' category. Vatsa, Savarna, and Bidah (or Garga in some lists).
19038
The official document that lists the 'Udedh' and confirms the 
marriage is permitted is known as the _____.
Asvajana-patra (or Siddhanta-patra)
19039
Which scholar's 'Tantra Vartika' provided an early reference to 
'Samuha Lekhya' (group records) similar to the Panji system?
Kumarila Bhatta.
19040
What geographical feature traditionally divides the 'Panch Gauda' 
from the 'Panch Dravida' Brahmins?
The Vindhya Mountains.
19041
In the Panji system, what happens if a person marries within their 
own Gotra?
The marriage is considered invalid (Sagotra marriage is prohibited).

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
19042
What is the purpose of the 'Dushana Panji'?
To record any flaws or violations of social/genealogical norms 
within a lineage.
19043
The 'Sakha Panji' system, which introduced more specific sub-
lineages, was expanded during the reign of which later King?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
19044
According to the text, why were the genealogical records moved 
from private family hands to state-appointed Panjikars?
Due to a decline in people's ability to maintain accurate 32-mula 
records, leading to accidental incestuous marriages.
19045
Which legendary mathematician and astronomer's lineage is 
mentioned in the Panji records?
Aryabhata.
19046
How many 'Chadvith' points (ancestral nodes) are calculated for a 
bride to ensure non-relationship with the groom?
16 points.
19047
In the context of the Panji records, what is the significance of the 
year 2009 AD mentioned in the title?
It marks the point up to which the modern merged Panji Prabandh 
analysis reaches.
19048
The concept of marriage eligibility based on 'Samana Pravara' 
means the bride and groom cannot share the same _____.
Ancestral sages (Pravaras)
19049
Under which specific condition can a marriage occur if a 'Chadvith' 
point matches between the groom's maternal side and the bride?
Marriage is permitted if the match is on the maternal side but 
prohibited if it is on the paternal side.
19050
Term: Samyaka
Definition: A high social status within the Panji system achieved 
through marriages that adhere to the highest purity standards.
19051
The Vajasaneyi Brahmins follow the rituals prescribed by the sage 
_____.
Yajnavalkya
19052
Which script was traditionally used to write the Panji records?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
19053
Which major Maithil poetâ€™s lineage is preserved in the Panji 
records for 19 generations?
Vidyapati Thakur.
19054
What is the total number of 'Shrotriya' Panjis recorded in the text?
32 Panjis.
19055
What is the total number of 'Yogya' and 'Panjibaddah' Panjis 
recorded in the text?
153 Panjis.
19056
The rule 'Matritah Panchami, Pitritah Saptami' refers to avoiding 
_____.
5 generations from the mother's side and 7 from the father's side.
19057
Which Panji record tracks the chronological expansion and 
migration of families from their original 'Mula Gram'?
Sakha Panji.
19058
How does the Panji system classify the status of a person whose 
ancestors were not originally recorded during Harisimhadeva's time?
They are often classified as 'Jaywal'.
19059
Concept: Pothi
Definition: The physical manuscript or book made of palm leaves 
(talapatra) or paper containing genealogical records.
19060
What must a Panjikar verify regarding the groom's mother's brother 
(maternal uncle)?
The groom cannot marry a girl from his maternal uncle's immediate 
family (Niman-matul rule).
19061
Why is the preservation of the Tirhuta script considered vital to the 
Panji system?
Because the vast majority of historical Panji manuscripts are written 
exclusively in that script.
19062
What was the primary purpose for the creation of the 'Panji 
Prabandh' system in Mithila?
To ensure caste purity by maintaining detailed genealogical records 
for marriage eligibility.
19063
Which three social groups were primarily included in the original 
Panji Prabandh system?
Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
19064
In the Panji system, what does the term 'Udegh' refer to?
The introduction of seven generations of ancestors required to 
verify a person's lineage.
19065
How many specific ancestral introductions are required for a 
complete 'Udegh' in the Brahmin community?
Thirty-two.
19066
According to the Panji rules for Shrotriya Brahmins, how many 
generations must be avoided on the father's side for marriage?
Seven.
19067
According to the Panji rules for Shrotriya Brahmins, how many 
generations must be avoided on the mother's side for marriage?
Five.
19068
What name is given to those who were excluded from the formal 
Panji records?
Jaibar.
19069
How many major 'Gotras' (clans) are identified within the Maithil 
Brahmin community according to the source?
Twenty.
19070
Which historical King of the Karnat dynasty is credited with the 
formalisation of the Panji system?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
19071
In which year (Shaka era and AD) was the Panji system reportedly 
organised under royal decree?
Shaka 1248 (1326 AD).
19072
What is the total number of 'Mools' (original roots/villages) 
identified for Brahmins in the system?
One hundred and sixty-seven.
19073
What are the two major Vedic branches (Veda and Shakha) 
followed by Maithil Brahmins?
Yajurveda (Madhyandini Shakha) and Samaveda (Kauthuma 
Shakha).
19074
What term is used for a Maithil Brahmin who follows the 
Samaveda?
Chandogya.
19075
What term is used for a Maithil Brahmin who follows the 
Yajurveda?
Vajasaneyi.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
19076
According to the source, why did Indian Brahmins eventually 
divide into ten regional groups?
Regional influences such as climate, language, diet, and attire 
caused cultural differentiation.
19077
The ten Brahmin groups are geographically divided into two broad 
categories known as _____ and _____.
Panch Gaur and Panch Dravid.
19078
What is the geographic boundary that separates the Panch Gaur 
from the Panch Dravid Brahmin groups?
The Vindhyachal Mountains.
19079
Which five Brahmin groups constitute the 'Panch Gaur'?
Utkal, Kanyakubja, Gaur, Maithil, and Saraswat.
19080
Which five Brahmin groups constitute the 'Panch Dravid'?
Karnat, Gujrat, Tailang, Dravid, and Maharashtra.
19081
In the context of Panji records, what is a 'Biji Purush'?
The earliest known ancestor or the progenitor of a specific lineage 
or clan.
19082
What is the difference between 'Mool' and 'Moolgram' in the Panji 
records?
Mool refers to the ancestral root village, while Moolgram refers to 
the current or later village of residence.
19083
Definition: Pravara
A lineage based on the specific Vedic Rishis who authored hymns 
in the Rigveda.
19084
What determines whether a family is classified as 'Tri-pravara'?
Having three ancestors who were authors of Rigvedic hymns.
19085
What determines whether a family is classified as 'Panch-pravara'?
Having five ancestors who were authors of Rigvedic hymns.
19086
Which three Gotras are specifically mentioned as having 'Panch-
pravara'?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Garga.
19087
What is the title of the specialist scholar responsible for maintaining 
and verifying Panji records?
Panjikar.
19088
The certificate of marriage eligibility issued by a Panjikar is known 
as _____.
Siddhanta Patra (or Aswajan Patra).
19089
Which philosopher's work 'Tantra Varttik' is cited as the earliest 
mention of maintaining lineage records (Samuh Lekhya)?
Kumarila Bhatta.
19090
What historical event in the 14th century prompted the formal royal 
management of Panji records?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya unknowingly married a close relative 
due to lack of lineage knowledge.
19091
How many main categories (Mool categories) were established 
during the reign of Harisimhadeva?
Thirty-five.
19092
Into which three hierarchical categories were the Brahmin Mools 
divided?
Ayanth Shrestha (First), Second Grade, and Madhyam (Middle).
19093
In the Shrotriya Panji, what is a 'Laukik'?
A specific social identity or sub-classification resulting from 
marriage alliances.
19094
What is 'Dushan Panji' used to record?
Flaws or lapses in lineage, such as prohibited marriages or social 
degradation, to protect future generations.
19095
Under whose reign did the 'Shakha Panji' (branch records) begin, 
leading to further social stratification?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
19096
What is the only Gotra found among the Karna Kayasthas of 
Mithila?
Kashyap.
19097
What does the term 'Chhadwith' signify in the calculation of 
marriage eligibility?
The sixth position in the genealogical tree, which acts as a 
prohibited degree of relationship.
19098
How many different 'Chhadwith' (sixth-position relationships) are 
checked for a bride's eligibility?
Sixteen.
19099
What is the 'Shandilya' Gotra's Pravara configuration?
Shandilya, Asit, and Deval (Tri-pravara).
19100
What is the 'Vatsa' Gotra's Pravara configuration?
Aurva, Chyavan, Bhargava, Jamadagnya, and Apnuvan (Panch-
pravara).
19101
What is the 'Kashyap' Gotra's Pravara configuration?
Kashyap, Avatsar, and Naidhruv (Tri-pravara).
19102
If a bride's Chhadwith ancestor is found in the groom's paternal 
side, is the marriage allowed?
No, it is prohibited.
19103
If a bride's Chhadwith ancestor appears in the groom's maternal 
side, is the marriage permitted?
Yes, provided the distance exceeds five generations.
19104
What script was traditionally used to write the Panji records?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
19105
What preservation method is suggested for Panji manuscripts 
according to the traditional verse quoted?
Protect from water, oil, and loose binding, and dry them in the sun 
when it enters Leo.
19106
What are 'Ayanth Shrestha' Mools?
The highest-ranking ancestral villages, including Kharaure, 
Khauare, and Budhabare.
19107
What does the source suggest is a modern scientific parallel to the 
Panji system?
Genome mapping or chromosome determination.
19108
What is the significance of 'Uteh' in Panji verification?
It is the detailed format/document used to check the 32 ancestors to 
establish marriage rights.
19109
The 'Dushan' or flaws recorded in the Panji include instances of 
marriage with which external groups?
Muslims and low-caste groups (e.g., Chamars or Rajakas).
19110
What is the 'Pari' in the context of the Panji records?
The descending rank or status of a lineage, which can decrease over 
time due to certain marriage choices.
19111
Which historical scholar is mentioned as being a descendant of the 
18th generation of the original Panjikar Gunakar Jha?
Modanand Jha.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
19112
How does the Panji system treat the children of an 'Unknown 
mother' (Agyat Matrika) historical record?
After several generations (usually six), the lineage is often 
considered purified and accepted into society.
19113
What is the role of the 'Siddhant Patra' in a Maithil wedding?
It is the formal written permission required before the wedding can 
proceed according to Shastra.
19114
Name one of the sixteen 'Chhadwith' positions listed in the rules.
The paternal great-grandfather's paternal grandfather (Vriddha 
Prapitamah Pitamah).
19115
What were the traditional materials used to write and bind Panji 
books?
Palm leaves (Talpatra), homemade ink, and wooden covers (Kastha 
Vatta).
19116
Process: How is a Panji book dried traditionally?
It is dried in the intense heat of the sun between roughly 16th 
August and 15th September.
19117
Term: Panji Prabandh
Definition: A comprehensive genealogical record system used in 
Mithila to ensure caste purity and verify marriage eligibility.
19118
Who was the Karnata dynasty ruler credited with formalising the 
Panji system in Mithila?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
19119
In which year AD was the Panji system officially established in 
Mithila?
1326 AD (Shaka 1248).
19120
Concept: Uderh
Definition: A detailed genealogical introduction covering 32 
lineages (maternal and paternal) required for marriage eligibility.
19121
The Panji system primarily maintains records for which three social 
groups in Mithila?
Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
19122
How many total 'Gotras' (lineages) are traditionally identified 
among Maithil Brahmins?
20
19123
Name the seven most prominent and well-populated Gotras among 
Maithil Brahmins.
Shandilya, Vatsa, Kashyapa, Savarna, Parashara, Bharadwaja, and 
Katyayana.
19124
Concept: Mula
Definition: The original village or ancestral 'root' of a specific 
family lineage within a Gotra.
19125
How many original 'Mula-grams' (root villages) were initially 
identified during the Panji Prabandh?
167
19126
How does 'Tri-pravara' differ from 'Pancha-pravara'?
Tri-pravara identifies three ancestral sages, while Pancha-pravara 
identifies five.
19127
According to the Panji system, marriage between the Vatsa and 
Savarna Gotras is forbidden for what reason?
They share the same common ancestors despite having different 
Gotra names.
19128
Which two major Vedic branches do Maithil Brahmins traditionally 
follow?
Yajurveda (Madhyandina branch) and Samaveda (Kauthuma 
branch).
19129
Maithil Brahmins following the Yajurveda are known by what term? Vajasaneyi.
19130
Maithil Brahmins following the Samaveda are known by what term? Chhandogya.
19131
The Panji system classifies Brahmins into which three primary 
hierarchical categories?
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddh.
19132
Term: Jaywar
Definition: Brahmins whose lineages were not initially registered in 
the Panji records.
19133
What is the standard marriage exclusion rule for the paternal side in 
the Panji system?
Avoidance of seven generations (later revised to six).
19134
What is the standard marriage exclusion rule for the maternal side 
in the Panji system?
Avoidance of five generations.
19135
What is the name of the certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming 
that a bride and groom are not related?
Siddhanta Patra (also known as Asvajanapatra).
19136
Who was appointed as the first chief Panjikar for Maithil Brahmins 
by Maharaja Harisimhadeva?
Gunakara Jha.
19137
Who was the first designated Panjikar for the Karna Kayastha 
community?
Shankardatta.
19138
Who was the first designated Panjikar for the Kshatriya community 
in Mithila?
Vijayadatta.
19139
The Puranas divide Indian Brahmins into two broad groups based 
on geography; name them.
Pancha Gauda (North) and Pancha Dravida (South).
19140
Into which of the 'Pancha Gauda' divisions do Maithil Brahmins 
fall?
Maithil.
19141
What geographical feature acts as the boundary between the Pancha 
Gauda and Pancha Dravida Brahmins?
The Vindhyachal mountains.
19142
Concept: Beeji Purush
Definition: The earliest known progenitor or founding ancestor of a 
specific family branch.
19143
Which 18th-century ruler introduced the 'Shakhe Panji' (branch 
records) to further categorize the Brahmin hierarchy?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
19144
How many Gotras are identified for the Karna Kayastha community 
in the source material?
One (Kashyapa).
19145
Which scholar is credited with revising the paternal marriage 
exclusion rule from seven to six generations?
Mahamaho-padhyaya Mahesh Thakur.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
19146
Concept: Chhadwith
Definition: The sixteen specific ancestor relationships that must be 
checked to confirm a bride and groom are not 'Sapinda' (kinsfolk).
19147
The Panji records are traditionally written in which script?
Tirhuta (also known as Mithilakshar).
19148
What traditional materials were used to preserve Panji records for 
centuries before digital imaging?
Palm leaves (Talapatra) and handmade ink.
19149
Why is the modern study of Panji often referred to as 'Genome 
Mapping'?
Because it tracks hereditary lineages and genetic ancestry over a 
1,500-year period.
19150
Which philosopher mentioned 'Samuha-Lekhya' (group records) as 
a way for families to preserve lineage in the 7th century?
Kumarila Bhatta.
19151
What is the religious significance of 'Pravara' in the Panji system?
It identifies the ancestors whose hymns are recited during sacrificial 
offerings (Yajna).
19152
Marriage into which side is permitted if the relationship appears in 
the 6th position but only on the maternal side?
The maternal side (Matripaksha).
19153
The Panji system explicitly forbids marriage with the children of 
which maternal relative?
The mother's brother (Maternal Uncle).
19154
Concept: Laukiki
Definition: Specific genealogical branch records used primarily by 
the Shrotriya category of Brahmins.
19155
In the Panji preservation verse, what three things must a book be 
protected from?
Water, oil, and loose binding.
19156
According to the text, what happened to a person who married their 
'Svajana' (prohibited kin)?
They were treated as outcasts (Chandala) and lost their Aryan status.
19157
Which major philosophical work by Kumarila Bhatta discusses the 
preservation of family records?
Tantra Vartika.
19158
How many main Mulas are identified as 'Ayanth Shrestha' (first 
category) in the Brahmin hierarchy?
13
19159
The text lists 'Dighare', 'Belonche', and 'Ekahate' as examples of 
which category of Mula?
Madhyam (Middle) Mula.
19160
What is the significance of the year 1248 Shaka in the context of 
Mithila's genealogy?
It is the traditional date for the large-scale formalisation of Panji 
records.
19161
Maithil Brahmins traditionally avoid marriages within the same 
Gotra, a practice known as _____.
Gotra Exogamy.
19162
The term 'Pancha Dravida' includes Brahmins from Utkala, Gurjara, 
Maharashtra, Karnataka, and _____.
Tailang (Andhra).
19163
What historical script is considered the 'soul' of the Panji Prabandh? Tirhuta script.
19164
Why were Panjikars required to verify 32 'Uderh' lineages before a 
marriage?
To ensure no accidental blood relation existed within the prohibited 
generational limits.
19165
Concept: Sakin
Definition: The current village of residence for a family branch, 
distinct from their original 'Mula'.
19166
Which group's Panji records are noted to have become largely 
extinct over time according to the text?
Kshatriyas.
19167
The Panji records contain historical notes on ancestors' educational 
titles and literary achievements; what does this indicate?
It serves as a cultural and intellectual history of the region beyond 
simple genealogy.
19168
In the Shrotriya hierarchy, what is the term for the four major 
genealogical divisions?
Chauvala (also known as categories/ranks).
19169
According to the source, why did geographical regionalism lead to 
the division of Brahmin groups?
Differences in climate, diet, language, and cultural practices 
isolated groups into specific regions.
19170
What is the primary difference in marriage rites between the 
Vajasaneyi and Chhandogya branches?
The Chhandogya marriage is divided into two parts (Purva and 
Uttara), whereas the Vajasaneyi has no such division.
19171
What specific celestial observation is required in a Chhandogya 
'Uttara Vivaha'?
Viewing the star Arundhati after sunset.
19172
How many generations of maternal ancestors were traditionally 
avoided for the 'Shrotriya' group specifically?
Five generations.
19173
Which Panji specifically documents lineages associated with 
Devadasis or unknown maternal roots?
The Dushana Panji (records of blemishes).
19174
Which historical ruler is credited with the formal establishment of 
the Panji Prabandh?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva of the Karnat dynasty.
19175
In what year (both Saka and AD) was the decision to formalise 
Panji records taken?
Saka 1248, which corresponds to 1326 AD.
19176
The term 'Udegh' refers to the introduction of how many 
generations for marriage eligibility?
Seven generations.
19177
How many ancestors are included in the comprehensive 'Udegh' 
introduction required for Shrotriya Brahmins?
Thirty-two ancestors.
19178
What are the three main hierarchical categories of Mithila Brahmins 
identified in the text?
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddh.
19179
What is the term for Brahmins in Mithila who are not formally 
registered in the Panji records?
Jaywar.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
19180
How many paternal generations must be avoided for marriage 
according to the rules for Shrotriya Brahmins?
Seven generations.
19181
How many maternal generations must be avoided for marriage for 
Shrotriya Brahmins?
Five generations.
19182
How many 'Gotras' (lineages) are traditionally recognised among 
Maithil Brahmins?
Twenty.
19183
How many 'Mula-Grams' (original villages) were identified during 
the formalisation of Panji records?
One hundred and sixty-seven.
19184
From the 167 original villages, how many 'Mukhya Mula' (primary 
roots) were specifically designated?
Thirty-four.
19185
In the context of 'Pravara', what is the difference between 'Tri-
pravara' and 'Pancha-pravara'?
Tri-pravara refers to three ancestral rishis who composed Rigvedic 
hymns, while Pancha-pravara refers to five.
19186
Name the three Gotras that are classified as 'Pancha-pravara'.
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharadwaja.
19187
What is the difference in Vedic branch affiliation between the 
'Yajurvedic' and 'Samavedic' Brahmins of Mithila?
Yajurvedic Brahmins follow the Madhyandina Shakha 
(Vajasaneyi), while Samavedic Brahmins follow the Kauthuma 
Shakha (Chandoga).
19188
According to the 'Pancha Gauda' classification, what are the five 
northern Brahmin groups?
Utkala, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, and Saraswat.
19189
The geographical divide between 'Pancha Gauda' and 'Pancha 
Dravida' Brahmins is the _____ mountain range.
Vindhyachal.
19190
What was the precursor to the Panji system mentioned in Kumarila 
Bhatta's 'Tantra Varttika'?
Samuh Lekhya (Collective Records).
19191
Who was the specific scholar appointed by King Harisimhadeva to 
manage the Maithil Brahmin Panji?
Gunakar Jha.
19192
Which subgroups in Mithila society, besides Brahmins, originally 
maintained Panji records?
Karna Kayasthas and Kshatriyas.
19193
What is the singular Gotra associated with all Karna Kayasthas?
Kashyapa.
19194
Define 'Biji Purush'.
The earliest known, identifiable ancestor from whom a specific 
lineage or 'Mula' descends.
19195
Which type of Panji record tracks lineages based on the 'Mula-
Gram' to identify descendants?
Mula Panji.
19196
The 16 ancestral verification points for a bride's eligibility are 
collectively called _____.
Chhadishta.
19197
According to the 'Chhadishta', the first point of check is the bride's 
_____.
Paternal Great-Great-Great Grandfather ($Vriddha-Prapitamah$).
19198
Under whose reign did the 'Shakha Panji' (branch records) and the 
Shrotriya-Yogya classification start to diverge significantly?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
19199
What is the script traditionally used to write Panji records?
Tirhuta (or Mithilakshar).
19200
What are the two major divisions of 'Pachhwari' Brahmins 
mentioned in the text?
Purbari-paat and Pachhwari-paat.
19201
In the Panji system, how many rishis comprise the 'Tri-pravara' of 
the Shandilya Gotra?
Three (Shandilya, Asit, and Deval).
19202
The rule 'Matritah Panchami Tyaktwa' means avoiding _____ 
generations from the mother's side.
Five.
19203
Which Panji specifically documents the ancestral rishis associated 
with a lineage?
Gotra Panji.
19204
The term 'Laukika' in the Panji system refers to the status or records 
of which specific rank?
Shrotriya.
19205
What is the 'sixteenth Chhadishta' point for a bride?
The maternal grandmother's maternal grandmother's maternal 
grandfather.
19206
According to the text, which scholar was the main examiner for the 
Panji Shastra in the time of Maharaja Kameshwar Singh?
Sir Ganganath Jha.
19207
How many 'Chhadishta' points are checked on the bride's side to 
ensure no overlap with the groom's paternal side?
Sixteen.
19208
What does 'Siddhanta' signify in the context of Maithil marriage?
The final official verification that the bride and groom are not 
closely related (non-Sapinda).
19209
Which Gotra includes the 'Mula' named 'Khauary' and 'Kharaury'?
Kashyapa.
19210
True or False: The text suggests that 'Genome Mapping' is a modern 
scientific parallel to the ancient Panji system.
True (it describes Panji as a historical form of genetic/genealogical 
tracking).
19211
The ancient scholar _____ is quoted regarding the importance of 
'Samuh Lekhya' for maintaining lineage purity.
Kumarila Bhatta.
19212
How many 'Mulas' does the Shandilya Gotra have according to the 
lists provided?
Approximately 44 (including Dighwa, Sarisab, Mahua, etc.).
19213
What is 'Ayanthi Shrestha'?
The first tier of the most respected 'Mula' roots, such as Kharaury 
and Khauary.
19214
Name one 'Madhyam Mula' listed in the text.
Dighway (or Belaunchay, Ekhatay).

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
19215
The rishi ancestors of the Bharadwaja Gotra are Bharadwaja, 
Angiras, and _____.
Barhaspatya.
19216
Which Gotra is associated with the rishis Shakti, Vashistha, and 
Parashar?
Parashar Gotra.
19217
What does the Panji term 'Adhogami' describe regarding lineage 
status?
The downward movement or degradation of genealogical rank due 
to marriage into lower tiers.
19218
Which scholar wrote 'Smritisar' and reportedly married an ineligible 
relative due to lack of records, prompting the formalisation of Panji?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya.
19219
What is the literal meaning of 'Panji' in this context?
A chronicle or register of names and lineages.
19220
In the provided lists, which Gotra does the 'Oini' Mula belong to?
Kashyapa.
19221
Which Mula is associated with the village 'Sarisab'?
Shandilya Gotra.
19222
What is the 'Shakha' of a Panji?
A detailed branch record of a specific family tree within a Mula.
19223
How many 'Chhadishta' points involve the bride's maternal 
grandfather ($Matamaha$)?
Four (points 9, 10, 11, and 12).
19224
Which rishi is the 'Biji Purush' for the Shandilya Gotra?
Shandilya.
19225
Who was appointed to manage the Panji of the Kshatriya (Rajput) 
community in Mithila?
Vijayadatta.
19226
Who was appointed to manage the Panji for the Karna Kayastha 
community?
Shankardatta.
19227
Why is the study of Tirhuta script essential for Panjikars?
Because the vast majority of historical Panji records are written 
exclusively in that script.
19228
The 'Pancha Dravida' Brahmins include those from Karnataka, 
Andhra (Telang), Dravida, Maharashtra, and _____.
Gujjar (Gujarat).
19229
What time of year is traditionally recommended to dry and preserve 
Panji books to prevent damage?
When the Sun enters the Leo ($Simha$) sign (mid-August to mid-
September).
19230
The term _____ refers to the specific genealogical 'fault' or history 
recorded to prevent future status issues.
Dushana.
19231
True or False: According to the text, marriage with a mother's 
brother's child is permitted in the Panji system.
False (it is specifically restricted as a forbidden relationship).
19232
How many generations must be avoided for marriage on the 
paternal side for 'Panjibaddh' Brahmins?
Six generations.
19233
What is the primary rishi for the 'Kashyapa' Gotra?
Kashyapa.
19234
Which Gotra is associated with the 'Kujouli' Mula?
Katyayana.
19235
In the genealogy of Vidyapati, how many generations are 
traditionally tracked before his birth?
Seven generations.
19236
What is the significance of the year 2000 AD in the provided text?
It marks the start of the digital imaging and transliteration project 
for the ancient Panji records.
19237
Which scholar is noted as the 'Biji' of the 'Sarisab' Mula?
Ratnapani.
19238
The term 'Mula-Gram' identifies the village of the _____.
Biji Purush (original ancestor).
19239
Which king requested the creation of the 'Shakha Panji' books in 
1760 AD?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
19240
How many hierarchical tiers ($Chaurals$) exist within the Shrotriya 
Panji?
Eight.
19241
The records for the 'Pachhwari' Brahmins contain how many Panjis? One hundred and fifty-three.
19242
Which Panji scholar was known as 'Panji Shastra Martand'?
Modanand Jha.
19243
Name the four rishis of the 'Vatsa' Gotra Pravara.
Aurva, Chyavana, Bhargava, Jamadagnya, and Apnavan.
19244
What was the primary purpose for the creation of the Maithili 'Panji 
Prabandh' system?
To ensure the genealogical purity and integrity of the Brahmin, 
Kayastha, and Kshatriya castes.
19245
Which Maithil ruler is credited with formalising the Panji system in 
$1326$ AD (Shaka $1248$)?
King Harisimhadeva.
19246
Who was the first designated 'Panjikar' for the Maithili Brahmin 
community?
Gunakar Jha.
19247
The Panji system defines 'Udhedh' as the detailed introduction of 
_____ generations of ancestors.
Seven
19248
To determine marriage eligibility, a Maithili Brahmin must provide 
the introduction of how many 'Kulas' (lineages)?
$32$
19249
In the context of the Panji system, what is a 'Biji Purush'?
The earliest known or foundational ancestor of a specific lineage.
19250
How many distinct 'Gotras' are recognized within the Maithili 
Brahmin community according to the Panji records?
$20$
19251
Which seven 'Gotras' are considered the most well-established and 
populous among Maithili Brahmins?
Shandilya, Vatsa, Kashyapa, Savarna, Parashara, Bharadwaja, and 
Katyayana.
19252
What is the specific meaning of 'Pravara' in the Maithili 
genealogical system?
Ancestors who were recognized as the original composers of Vedic 
hymns.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
19253
How does a 'Tri-pravara' lineage differ from a 'Pancha-pravara' 
lineage?
A Tri-pravara lineage has three recognized hymn-composing 
ancestors, while a Pancha-pravara has five.
19254
The original village associated with a specific lineage is known in 
the Panji system as the _____.
Mulgram
19255
How many 'Mulgrams' (original villages) were initially identified 
for the Maithili Brahmin community?
$167$
19256
In Maithil Brahmin hierarchy, which category is considered the 
highest following the Panji division?
Shrotriya
19257
Those who were not officially recorded in the Panji system were 
categorised as _____.
Jaybar
19258
According to Dharmashastra rules mentioned in the Panji, how 
many generations must be avoided on the fatherâ€™s side for 
marriage?
Seven (often revised to six in practice).
19259
How many generations must be avoided on the mother's side for a 
marriage to be considered valid under Panji rules?
Five
19260
What is the 'Siddhanta Patra' (or 'Asvajana Patra') in the Panji 
system?
A certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that a bride and groom 
are not prohibited relatives.
19261
Which script was traditionally used to record and maintain the Panji 
genealogical records?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
19262
Under which Maithil ruler did the 'Shakha Panji' (branch records) 
and the Shrotriya hierarchy divisions begin?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
19263
What does a 'Dushan Panji' record?
Defects, illicit relations, or lineage 'stains' that occurred within a 
family history.
19264
The Panji system classifies $35$ primary lineages into three 
categories: Ayanti Shrestha, Dvitiya Shrestha, and _____.
Madhyam Mula
19265
Why is marriage prohibited between the Savarna and Vatsa Gotras 
despite them being different names?
They share the same set of five Pravara ancestors.
19266
What are the two main Vedic branches (Shakhas) followed by 
Maithili Brahmins?
Yajurveda (Madhyandina Shaka) and Samaveda (Kauthuma Shaka).
19267
Brahmins following the Kauthuma branch of Samaveda are referred 
to as _____.
Chhandogya
19268
Brahmins following the Madhyandina branch of Yajurveda are 
referred to as _____.
Vajasaneyi
19269
In the Panji system, what does the term 'Chhattha' (Sixth) refer to in 
the context of marriage checks?
The sixteen specific ancestral positions where overlap between 
bride and groom is prohibited.
19270
If an ancestral overlap occurs in the 'Chhattha' position on the 
groom's maternal side, is the marriage allowed?
Yes, as the prohibition applies strictly to the paternal side overlap.
19271
What historical event led to the institutionalisation of Panjikars as 
state-appointed record keepers?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya unknowingly married his first cousin 
due to a lack of genealogical records.
19272
What was the significance of the 'Samuh Lekhya' mentioned by 
Kumarila Bhatta in the $7$th century?
It was an early form of collective family records kept to protect 
lineage integrity.
19273
How many 'Pancha Gauda' Brahmin groups are there, and what is 
the Maithil group's position among them?
There are five groups; Maithil is one of the five Northern (Pancha 
Gauda) Brahmin groups.
19274
What geographical feature traditionally divides the 'Pancha Gauda' 
and 'Pancha Dravida' Brahmin groups?
The Vindhyachal mountain range.
19275
The 'Gotra Panji' contains a list of all Gotras and their _____.
Ancient Mulas (lineages).
19276
Term: Paanj
Definition: The official genealogical record books maintained by 
the Panjikars.
19277
How does the marriage ritual of 'Uttara Vivaha' differ between 
Chhandogya and Vajasaneyi Brahmins?
Chhandogya weddings involve seeing the Arundhati star after 
sunset, while Vajasaneyi weddings have no such requirement.
19278
Which Panji type specifically lists the 'Sapinda' (lineal) relationship 
to ensure religious and marital compliance?
Udhedh Panji
19279
The title 'Paanjikar' literally refers to an expert who _____.
Manages and verifies the Panji records for marriage eligibility.
19280
Which century marked the beginning of the written Panji system as 
commissioned by the state?
The $14$th century.
19281
Under what condition is marriage between the bride and groom 
forbidden regarding their maternal uncles?
The groom cannot marry the daughter of his maternal uncle.
19282
What is the 'Laukik' in the context of the Shrotriya Panji system?
The social standing or 'status' level within the Shrotriya community, 
which can decrease over generations.
19283
Identify the four Sanskaras (rituals) performed during the 
Upanayana of a Chhandogya Brahmin.
Namakarana, Chudakarana, Upanayana, and Samavartana.
19284
In the Panji terminology, 'Pachhwari Paat' refers to which group of 
Brahmins?
The Non-Shrotriya Brahmins (Yogya and Panjibaddha).
19285
What does a 'Patra Panji' primarily record?
The specific Mulgrams (villages) of residence, starting from 
approximately $500$ years ago.
19286
What happens to a Shrotriya's rank if they marry into a lower-
ranked Panjibaddha family?
Their rank decreases, potentially causing the family to move out of 
the top hierarchy over time.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
19287
True or False: The Panji system was historically used by Maithil 
Kshatriyas (Rajputs) as well as Brahmins.
True
19288
Which Panjikar was appointed by King Harisimhadeva specifically 
for the Karna Kayastha community?
Shankardatta
19289
The term 'Jaybar' describes individuals who are 'Panjibaddha' but 
have _____.
Lost their official genealogical records or eligibility status over time.
19290
What is the minimum number of ancestors required for an 'Udhedh' 
check for a bride?
$16$ (representing the 16 'Chhattha' positions).
19291
How does the Panji system track 'Pravas' (migration)?
By recording both the 'Mula' (original village) and the current 'Dih' 
(settlement village).
19292
According to the text, what environmental factor led to the 
preservation of Panji books in the month of Bhadrapada?
The intense heat (sunny days) used to dry and protect the palm-leaf 
manuscripts from moisture.
19293
The verse starting with 'Tarkabdhi Shunyam' records the coronation 
or arrival of which king?
Nanyadeva (founder of the Karnata dynasty).
19294
Why is the Maithili Panji system often compared to 'Genome 
Mapping' in a modern context?
Because it acts as a detailed hereditary database tracking genetic 
lineages over $1000$ years.
19295
What is the significance of 'Tantravarttika' in the history of the 
Panji system?
It is an ancient text by Kumarila Bhatta that validates the necessity 
of keeping caste records.
19296
What does the Panji record indicate about social mobility, as 
evidenced by 'Dushan' records?
It shows that while purity was prioritized, cases of inter-caste or 
irregular marriages were documented to track their impact on 
lineage.
19297
Identify the primary 'Mula' category to which the Khadaurey and 
Khauary lineages belong.
Ayanti Shrestha (First Grade).
19298
Who was the 'Biji Purush' of the Mandar Mula, a prominent lineage 
mentioned in the text?
Mahamahopadhyaya Trivitram Bhatta.
19299
In the Panji system, the groom is referred to as 'Vara' and the bride 
as _____.
Kanya
19300
The 'Chhattha' rule for marriage eligibility focuses on avoiding 
ancestors within _____ degrees of separation.
Six
19301
What type of marriage was considered so severe that the offspring 
were labelled 'Chandala' and excluded from Aryan dignity?
Marriage within the prohibited 'Svajana' (close kin) boundaries.
19302
According to Maharaja Mahesh Thakur's reform, the seventh 
generation on the father's side became _____ for marriage.
Acceptable (Grahya).
19303
During which historical period was the Panji Prabandha system 
officially established?
The medieval period, specifically around $1326$ AD ($1248$ 
Shaka).
19304
Which ruler is credited with the formal organisation and promotion 
of the Panji system?
King Harisimhadeva of the Karnata dynasty.
19305
In the context of Panji, what does the term 'Udeah' refer to?
The detailed introduction and identification of $32$ specific 
ancestors.
19306
According to traditional Maithil Brahmin marriage rules, how many 
generations must be avoided on the father's side?
Seven generations.
19307
For marriage eligibility in the Panji system, how many generations 
must be avoided on the mother's side?
Five generations.
19308
Name the three main social categories of Maithil Brahmins created 
through the Panji system.
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddha.
19309
What status is given to those who are not recorded in the official 
Panji registers?
They are categorised as 'Jaybar'.
19310
How many primary 'Gotras' are recognised within the Maithil 
Brahmin community according to the text?
Twenty.
19311
What is a 'Mool' in the Panji system?
The original ancestral village or place of origin of a lineage.
19312
How many primary 'Mools' ($Mukhya \ Mool$) were initially 
determined in the system?
Thirty-four, later expanded to $35$.
19313
What is the total number of 'Moolgrams' (ancestral villages) 
identified in the Maithil Brahmin records?
One hundred and sixty-seven.
19314
In the Panji system, what does the term 'Pravara' signify?
The specific ancestors who were composers of Vedic hymns 
($Suktas$).
19315
What is the difference between a 'Tri-pravara' and a 'Pancha-
pravara' lineage?
Tri-pravara names three hymn-composing ancestors, while Pancha-
pravara names five.
19316
Maithil Brahmins following the Shukla Yajurveda are known as 
_____.
Vajasaneyi.
19317
What is a 'Siddhanta Patra' (or 'Asvajana Patra') in the Panji 
tradition?
A formal certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that a bride 
and groom are not prohibited relations.
19318
What is the professional title of the genealogists who manage and 
verify the Panji records?
Panjikar.
19319
According to the 'Tantra Varttika' by Kumarila Bhatta, what was 
the purpose of 'Samuha-lekhya'?
To record family lineages to prevent the loss of ancestral history 
and protect caste purity.
19320
Who was the first Panjikar appointed specifically for the Maithil 
Brahmins?
Gunaakar Jha.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
19321
Which Gotra is exclusively found among the Karna Kayastha 
community according to the text?
Kashyapa.
19322
In the Panji classification, what is the 'Biji Purush'?
The earliest known progenitor or founding ancestor of a specific 
lineage.
19323
Which Puranic division of Brahmins does the Maithil community 
belong to?
Pancha Gauda.
19324
Name the five groups that constitute the 'Pancha Gauda' Brahmins.
Utkala, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, and Saraswat.
19325
What geographical feature serves as the boundary between Pancha 
Gauda and Pancha Dravida Brahmins?
The Vindhyachal mountain range.
19326
Which Gotras are specifically mentioned as having five ancestors in 
their Pravara (Pancha-pravara)?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Garga.
19327
Marriage between Vatsa and Savarna Gotras is prohibited because 
they share the same _____.
Ancestors (Pravara).
19328
How many grades or 'Chauras' (categories) exist within the 
Shrotriya Brahmin Panji?
Eight.
19329
What is 'Dushana Panji' used for in the genealogical records?
To record flaws or 'blemishes' in a lineage, such as prohibited 
marriages or social transgressions.
19330
Under whose reign was the 'Shakha Panji' (branch records) 
introduced?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
19331
What is the significance of 'Laukik' in the Shrotriya Panji system?
It refers to the specific lineages and their relative social standing 
within the Shrotriya category.
19332
What is the 'Chadhua-karan' ceremony in the context of Maithil 
Brahmin sacraments?
The tonsure or first hair-cutting ceremony.
19333
Which two sacraments are part of the 'Uttar Vivah' for Chhandoga 
Brahmins but not necessarily Vajasaneyi?
Sunset marriage rituals and viewing the star Arundhati.
19334
The rule 'Matritah Panchami, Pitritah Saptami' refers to avoiding 
_____ generations of relatives.
Five maternal and seven paternal.
19335
What is 'Sarpindi' or 'Sapinda' relationship in the context of 
marriage laws?
Shared bloodline or ancestral connection within a specific number 
of generations.
19336
Which historical document first mentions 'Samuha Lekhya' (family 
group records) in the $7^{th}$ century?
The 'Tantra Varttika' by Kumarila Bhatta.
19337
What is the 'Adhikaar' verification process performed before a 
Maithil wedding?
A check by the Panjikar to ensure the bride and groom have no 
prohibited genealogical overlaps.
19338
The 'Brahmapura' Mool is unique because it is found in _____ 
different Gotras.
Six.
19339
What is the status of a child born from a marriage with a 'Svajana' 
(prohibited relative)?
The child was traditionally considered 'Chandal' or socially 
excommunicated.
19340
Which Maithil Brahmin category is historically linked to the 
'Pachhwari' (Western) division?
The non-Shrotriya Brahmins (Yogya and Vamshaj).
19341
How often were the Panji palm-leaf manuscripts traditionally dried 
to prevent decay?
Annually, when the Sun is in the Leo ($Simha$) zodiac sign (mid-
August to mid-September).
19342
What does the 'Shakha Panji' track compared to the 'Mool Panji'?
Shakha Panji tracks specific branches and individual descendants, 
while Mool tracks the original root lineages.
19343
What is a 'Jaybar' Brahmin?
A Brahmin whose ancestors' details were not recorded during the 
official Panji census.
19344
According to the text, why did Panji records eventually stop being 
maintained for the Kshatriya community?
Due to the passage of time and lack of systematic institutional 
support.
19345
In the Pravara list for the Sandilya Gotra, who are the three 
ancestors?
Sandilya, Asita, and Devala.
19346
Which Gotra includes 'Shakti', 'Vashistha', and 'Parashara' in its 
Pravara?
Parashara.
19347
The 'Dushana' records occasionally note marriages with 'Devadasis'. 
What does this imply?
That the lineage had integrated descendants from non-Brahmin 
temple dancers.
19348
What does 'Anatishay' or 'Anatishakti' refer to in the context of 
marriage eligibility?
The absence of any prohibited genealogical connection between two 
families.
19349
What is the total number of ancestors involved in the 'Udeah' check 
for both bride and groom?
Thirty-two.
19350
Which category of Maithil Brahmin is considered the highest in the 
social hierarchy established by the Panji?
Shrotriya.
19351
The Panji system was a response to which event involving Pandit 
Harinath Upadhyaya?
His accidental marriage to a prohibited relative (his first cousin's 
daughter) due to lack of records.
19352
What is the 'Siddhanta' in a Maithil marriage ceremony?
The final approval and certification of eligibility given by the 
Panjikars.
19353
Name the two primary branches of the Maithil community that still 
strictly maintain Panji records.
Maithil Brahmins and Karna Kayasthas.
19354
In the Panji system, what happens if a person marries someone 
from the same Gotra?
The marriage is considered invalid ($Sagotra \ Vivah$ prohibition).
19355
The term 'Moolgram' literally means _____.
Root village.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
19356
How many ancestors are traced in the maternal line 
($Matripaksha$) for a Yogya Brahmin marriage?
Five generations.
19357
The Panji system was intended to preserve 'Jati Shuddhi', which 
means _____.
Caste purity.
19358
Which scholar's lineage is used as a prime example of 'Shakha 
Panji' continuity?
Vidyapati Thakur.
19359
What role does the 'Biji Purush' play in identifying a 'Mool'?
He is the definitive ancestor whose settlement in a village 
established the 'Mool' name.
19360
What historical event in $1326$ AD led to the formalisation of the 
Panji?
The gathering of scholars and King Harisimhadeva's decree to 
record all lineages.
19361
Which Gotra has 'Kashyapa', 'Avatsara', and 'Naidhruva' in its 
Pravara?
Kashyapa.
19362
The 'Pancha Dravida' Brahmins live south of the _____ mountains.
Vindhya.
19363
What is the 'Ayaanti Shrestha' category in the Mool classification?
The first or highest grade of ancestral villages ($Mool$).
19364
How many Mools are classified under the 'Ayaanti Shrestha' (First 
Category)?
Thirteen.
19365
Name the second category of Mools in the Brahmin hierarchy.
Dwadvitiya (Second Grade).
19366
What is 'Madhyam Mool'?
The intermediate or middle category of ancestral villages.
19367
How many Mools are listed as 'Madhyam'?
Eight.
19368
The 'Karnata' dynasty, associated with the Panji, ruled over which 
region?
Mithila (Videha).
19369
What is the primary Vedic affiliation of the 'Vajasaneyi' Brahmins?
Shukla Yajurveda ($Madhyandina$ branch).
19370
The 'Chhandoga' Brahmins follow the 'Kauthuma' branch of which 
Veda?
Samaveda.
19371
Why is the star Arundhati significant in Maithil Brahmin weddings? Chhandoga Brahmins must view it after sunset as part of their 
marriage ritual.
19372
What is 'Pancha-Siddhanta' in the context of genealogical rules?
The five generation avoidance rule on the maternal side.
19373
The 'Panji Prabandha' is often described as a form of ancient 
'Genome Mapping' because it _____.
Systematically records biological lineages to prevent inbreeding and 
maintain hereditary traits.
19374
What is the primary purpose of the 'Panji Prabandh' system 
established in Mithila?
To maintain caste purity and document genealogy for determining 
marriage eligibility.
19375
According to the source, what is the historical range covered by the 
Mithila Panji records?
The records span from $450$ AD to $2009$ AD.
19376
Which ruler is credited with the formal initiation and 
institutionalisation of the Panji system in $1326$ AD?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva of the Karnata dynasty.
19377
The Panji Prabandh was originally created for which three specific 
social groups in Mithila?
Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
19378
What does the term 'Udhaedh' signify in the context of Maithil 
genealogy?
The introduction and verification of seven generations of ancestors 
to establish lineage purity.
19379
For marriage eligibility, how many generations must be excluded 
(avoided) on the paternal side for a 'Shrotriya' Brahmin?
$7$ generations.
19380
For marriage eligibility, how many generations must be excluded 
(avoided) on the maternal side for a 'Shrotriya' Brahmin?
$5$ generations.
19381
How does the paternal exclusion rule differ for 'Panjibaddhas' 
compared to 'Shrotriyas'?
Panjibaddhas must exclude $6$ generations on the paternal side 
instead of $7$.
19382
Into which four hierarchical categories were Maithil Brahmins 
divided following the Panji system's implementation?
Shrotriya, Yogya, Panjibaddha, and Jaibar.
19383
How many 'Gotras' (clans) are documented among the Maithil 
Brahmins in the Panji system?
$20$ Gotras.
19384
In the Panji system, how many 'Mools' (original ancestral villages) 
are identified as primary?
$167$ Mools.
19385
The 'Pancha Gauda' and 'Pancha Dravida' categories divide the total 
Brahmin community of India into how many regional groups?
$10$ groups.
19386
To which larger regional division of Brahmins do the 'Maithil 
Brahmins' belong?
The Pancha Gauda group.
19387
What is the specific branch (shakha) of the Yajurveda followed by 
'Vajasaneyi' Maithil Brahmins?
The Madhyandina branch.
19388
Maithil Brahmins who follow the Samaveda Kauthuma branch are 
known as _____.
Chhandoga.
19389
How many 'Pravaras' (ancestral lineages within a Gotra) are 
typically recognized in Maithil Brahmin families?
They are divided into Tri-pravara ($3$ ancestors) and Panch-
pravara ($5$ ancestors).
19390
Which three Gotras among Maithil Brahmins are categorized as 
'Panch-pravara'?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharva.
19391
Who was the scholar appointed by Maharaja Harisimhadeva to 
manage the Brahmin Panji records?
Pandit Gunaikar Jha.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
19392
Who was appointed to manage the Panji records for the 'Karna 
Kayasthas'?
Shankardatta.
19393
In Panji terminology, who is the 'Bijee Purusha'?
The earliest known ancestor from whom a specific lineage or 'Mool' 
originates.
19394
What is 'Mool Gram' in the context of Mithila genealogy?
The original village or ancestral seat associated with the Bijee 
Purusha of a clan.
19395
What is the function of 'Siddhanta Patra' (or 'Asvajan Patra') issued 
by a Panjikar?
It is a legal/religious certificate confirming that a bride and groom 
are not related within prohibited degrees.
19396
The rule 'Matritah Panchami, Pitritah Saptami' refers to the 
exclusion of how many generations from the mother and father 
respectively?
Five generations from the mother and seven from the father.
19397
What specific genealogical information does the 'Dushan Panji' 
record?
It documents social flaws, 'kshana' (irregularities), or prohibited 
marriages in a lineage.
19398
Which Panji type provides a detailed record of the branches and 
sub-branches of various families?
Shakha Panji.
19399
The $16$ ancestral checkpoints used to determine marriage 
eligibility are collectively known as _____.
Chhadishta.
19400
Who introduced the 'Shakha Panji' system and the hierarchical 
classifications of Shrotriya and others?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
19401
What is the primary language and script used in traditional Panji 
manuscripts?
Maithili language in the Tirhuta (Mithilakshar) script.
19402
According to the 'Panji Prabandh', what defines a person as 
'Panjibaddha'?
A person whose name and lineage are officially recorded in the 
Panji books.
19403
Why were the Gotras 'Vatsa' and 'Savarna' traditionally prohibited 
from intermarrying despite having different names?
They share the same common ancestors (Pravaras).
19404
What is the 'Mool' of the Maithil poet Vidyapati as per the Panji 
records?
Bisaphi (from the Kashyap Gotra).
19405
Which ancient philosopher's work, 'Tantra Varttika', mentions 
'Samuha Lekhya' as a precursor to the Panji system?
Kumarila Bhatta.
19406
What is the religious significance of 'Pravara' in Vedic rituals for 
Maithil Brahmins?
It names the specific sages who composed the Vedic hymns for that 
lineage's branch.
19407
What manual verification must a Panjikar perform before a 
marriage is sanctioned?
Verification of $32$ ancestral 'Mools' to ensure no Sapinda 
relationship exists.
19408
In the context of historical Mithila, what led to the regional 
differentiation of Brahmins according to the text?
Regional differences in climate, language, diet, and dress affected 
local customs and practices.
19409
What are the four 'Samskaras' (sacraments) observed by Chhandoga 
Brahmins in Mithila?
Namakarana, Chudakarana, Upanayana, and Samavartana.
19410
How does the 'Vajasaneyi' marriage ritual timing differ from the 
'Chhandoga' practice?
Vajasaneyi marriages can occur day or night, while Chhandoga 
marriages have specific segments, including seeing the Arundhati 
star after sunset.
19411
In the classification of Mools, which category is considered 'Ayanti 
Shrestha' (first class)?
Lineages like Khadaure, Khauade, Budhabade, and Madariye.
19412
Which Gotra includes the Mools of Dighwe, Sarisab, Mahua, and 
Pabauli?
Sandilya Gotra.
19413
Which Gotra includes the Mools of Pali, Hariambe, and Takwal?
Vatsa Gotra.
19414
The Mools of Oini, Khaual, and Mandar belong to which Gotra?
Kashyap Gotra.
19415
Which Mool is unique for appearing across several Gotras including 
Sandilya, Vatsa, and Kashyap?
Brahmpura.
19416
The 'Tri-pravara' sages for the Sandilya Gotra are Sandilya, Asita, 
and _____.
Devala.
19417
The 'Panch-pravara' sages for the Vatsa Gotra include Aurva, 
Chyavana, Bhargava, Jamadagnya, and _____.
Apnuvana.
19418
What does 'Sapinda' relationship mean in the context of marriage 
laws?
It refers to individuals who share common ancestors within a 
specific number of generations and are thus barred from marrying.
19419
According to the text, what is the consequence of marrying within a 
'Svajan' (prohibited relation)?
The offspring were historically considered 'Chandala' and excluded 
from Aryan social status.
19420
Which ruler's reign saw the decline of Panji among Mithila's 
Rajputs/Kshatriyas?
The records suggest it happened over time, but Rajputs still follow 
village-based exclusion (avoiding paternal/maternal villages).
19421
Which Panji version specifically lists the 'Dosh' (vices/flaws) to 
help lineages avoid past negative influences?
Dushan Panji.
19422
What is the significance of the $16$th 'Chhadishta' (Mother's 
maternal grandmother's maternal grandmother)?
It is the final ancestral line checked to ensure total genealogical 
separation.
19423
What is the meaning of the term 'Jaibar' (or Jayabar) in the Mithila 
social hierarchy?
Brahmins who are not officially registered or 'Panjibaddha' in the 
genealogical records.
19424
The 'Pravara' for the Bharadvaj Gotra consists of Bharadvaj, 
Angiras, and _____.
Barhaspatya.
19425
According to the Panji system, what determines the 'Mool' of an 
individual?
The original ancestral village where the family's 'Bijee Purusha' 
resided.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
19426
Which Panjikar was assigned to handle the genealogy for the 
'Kshatriya' (Rajput) caste?
Vijaydatta.
19427
The text compares the Panji system to which modern biological 
concept?
Genome Mapping.
19428
What happened to the status of a lineage in the Panji system if they 
married into a 'lower' category family?
The family status ('Paanti') would decline (Adhogami).
19429
Which month is traditionally recommended for drying and 
maintaining Panji books to prevent decay?
The month of Bhadra (mid-August to mid-September) when the sun 
is in Leo.
19430
Who was the first 'Panji-shashtra Martand' title holder mentioned in 
the text?
Modanand Jha.
19431
Which Brahmin category is also referred to as 'Pubari-paanti'?
The Shrotriyas.
19432
How many 'Chaudalas' (categories/levels) exist within the Shrotriya 
Panji?
$8$ levels.
19433
How many Panji books are currently associated with the 'Yogya' 
and 'Panjibaddha' categories combined?
$153$ Panjis.
19434
What is the primary difference in the 'Upanayana' (thread 
ceremony) between the two Veda branches in Mithila?
Chhandogas perform $4$ specific sacraments, while Vajasaneyis 
perform $4$ different ones including 'Vedarambha'.
19435
Term: 'Mool Panji'
The fundamental genealogical book recording the basic lineages 
and ancestral origins.
19436
The Mool 'Belonche' belongs to which Gotra?
Bharadvaj Gotra.
19437
What is recorded in the 'Udhaedh Panji' specifically?
The 'Sapinda' relationship and ancestral lineage of the last $6$ 
generations.
19438
Which Gotra is the only one identified for the 'Karna Kayasthas' in 
Mithila?
Kashyap Gotra.
19439
In the Panji system, what is the role of a 'Panjikar'?
A professional genealogist authorised by the state to maintain 
records and verify marriage eligibility.
19440
Which Mithila king's court included Gunaikar Jha, the architect of 
the Panji records?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
19441
How many total Panjis are mentioned for 'Shrotriyas'?
$32$ Panjis.
19442
The principle 'Panchamat Saptamat Urdhvam' suggests marriage is 
allowed after which generations?
After the $5$th generation from the mother and the $7$th from the 
father.
19443
Which document is considered the 'Soul' of the Panji system and 
must be produced before any wedding?
The 'Siddhanta Patra' (Eligibility Certificate).
19444
True or False: The Panji system allows marriage if the bride and 
groom belong to the same Gotra but different Mools.
