avoided on the maternal side for a marriage to be valid?
Five generations ($5$).
19996
Into which three main hierarchical categories were the Maithil 
Brahmins divided following the Panji implementation?
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddha.
19997
What happened to those Brahmins who were not recorded in the 
Panji system?
They were classified as 'Jaybar'.
19998
How many primary 'Moolas' (original lineages) are identified as the 
most significant out of the $167$ recorded?
Thirty-four ($34$), later increased to $35$ with the addition of the 
'Khauam' branch.
19999
The two main Vedic divisions of Maithil Brahmins based on their 
ritual practices are Chhandogya and _____.
Vajasaneyi
20000
Which Vedic branch do the 'Vajasaneyi' Brahmins follow?
The Shukla Yajurveda (Madhyandina Shakha).
20001
Which Vedic branch do the 'Chhandogya' Brahmins follow?
The Samaveda (Kauthuma Shakha).
20002
What are the two types of 'Pravara' families found among Maithil 
Brahmins?
Tri-pravara (three ancestors) and Panch-pravara (five ancestors).
20003
In the Panji system, the term _____ refers to the earliest known 
ancestor of a specific lineage.
Beeji Purusha
20004
The term _____ refers to the original ancestral village associated 
with a particular lineage in the Panji records.
Moolagrama
20005
Which historical King of the Karnata dynasty is credited with the 
formal initiation of the Panji Prabandh in $1326$ A.D.?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
20006
Who was the first designated 'Panjikar' (registrar) appointed for the 
Maithil Brahmins by Maharaja Harisimhadeva?
Pandit Gunakar Jha.
20007
Who was the first designated 'Panjikar' for the Karna Kayastha 
community?
Shankaradatta.
20008
Who was the first designated 'Panjikar' for the Kshatriya 
community?
Vijayadatta.
20009
The 'Pancha-Gauda' Brahmin group consists of Utkala, 
Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, and _____.
Saraswat
20010
Geographically, the 'Pancha-Gauda' Brahmins are located to the 
_____ of the Vindhyachal mountains.
North
20011
What is the 'Siddhanta-patra' (also known as Asvajana-patra) in the 
Mithila marriage tradition?
A certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that the bride and 
groom are not 'Svajana' (prohibited relatives).
20012
In the Maithil Brahmin Gotra list, which two Gotras are prohibited 
from intermarrying because they share ancestors?
Vatsa and Savarna.
20013
Which Maharaja of Darbhanga ordered the creation of specific 
'Sakha Panjis' for the Shrotriya, Jogya, and Panjibaddha categories?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
20014
According to the 'Tantra Vartika' by Kumarila Bhatta, what was the 
purpose of 'Samuha-lekhya'?
To record the traditions of ancestors to ensure the preservation of 
lineage and avoid forgetting family history.
20015
Under the rules of Maharaja Mahesh Thakur, the paternal side 
prohibition for marriage was modified from seven to _____ 
generations.
Six ($6$).
20016
How many 'Shadvitha' (prohibited ancestral connections) are 
analyzed by a Panjikar for every marriage candidate?
Sixteen ($16$).
20017
What does 'Svajana' mean in the context of Mithila's marriage laws? A person who is within the prohibited degree of kinship and thus 
ineligible for marriage.
20018
The Karna Kayastha community in Mithila is associated with only 
one Gotra, which is _____.
Kashyapa
20019
Which Gotra includes the Moolas of 'Dighwa' (Dighore), 'Sarisab', 
and 'Mahua'?
Shandilya.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
20020
Which Gotra includes the Moolas of 'Pali', 'Hariambe', and 
'Tarisuni'?
Vatsa.
20021
Which Gotra includes the Moolas of 'Oini', 'Khaual', and 'Mandar'?
Kashyapa.
20022
Identify the Gotra associated with the 'Ekahara' and 'Belauch' 
Moolas.
Bharadwaja.
20023
Identify the Gotra associated with the 'Kujoli' and 'Jalki' Moolas.
Katyayana.
20024
What historical event in the $14^{th}$ century led to the 
formalisation of the Panji system?
The marriage of Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya to a relative (his 
father's brother's daughter's daughter) due to genealogical ignorance.
20025
In the Panji system, if an individual is not found in the registers, 
they are collectively referred to as _____.
Pachhbaripar (or Pachhbariba).
20026
How many total 'Panjis' (registers) are recorded for the Shrotriya 
category?
Thirty-two ($32$).
20027
How many total 'Panjis' are recorded for the Yogya and 
Panjibaddha categories combined?
One hundred and fifty-three ($153$).
20028
The 'Varna Ratnakara' mentions $72$ Rajput clans, of which 
detailed descriptions are provided for _____.
Sixty-four ($64$).
20029
Concept: Paati Panji
A register that mentions the current Moolagrama (village of 
residence) and has been in use for about $500$ years.
20030
Who was the scholar 'Modanand Jha'?
A renowned Panjikar who received the title 'Panji Shastra Martand' 
from Maharaja Kameshwar Singh.
20031
What does the mnemonic 'Jalat Raksha, Tailat Raksha' signify for 
Panjikars?
Instructions to protect the palm-leaf manuscripts from water, oil, 
and loose binding.
20032
Which celestial event is traditionally considered the best time to dry 
and preserve Panji manuscripts?
When the Sun is in the Leo (Simha) constellation (approx. $16$ 
August to $15$ September).
20033
Term: Sapaná¸ a
Definition: Relatives who share a common ancestral body or offer 
funeral cakes to the same ancestor.
20034
If a bride is the $6^{th}$ generation descendant from a common 
ancestor, she is referred to as _____ in the Panji check.
Shadvitha (Chhadvitha).
20035
True or False: According to the Panji rules, a marriage can occur if 
a common ancestor appears in the maternal side of the groom at the 
$6^{th}$ generation.
True (it is prohibited only up to the $5^{th}$ generation on the 
maternal side).
20036
True or False: A marriage is prohibited if a common ancestor 
appears in the paternal side of the groom at the $6^{th}$ generation.
True (it is prohibited up to the $7^{th}$ generation, though 
modified to $6$ in some contexts).
20037
Which sage is associated with the 'Katyayana' Gotra?
Vishnu and Angiras.
20038
Which sage is associated with the 'Shandilya' Gotra?
Asit and Deval.
20039
Which sages are associated with the 'Panch-pravara' Vatsa Gotra?
Aurva, Chyavana, Bhargava, Jamadagnya, and Apnavan.
20040
The rule 'Matritah Panchami Tyaktwa Pitritah Saptami Bhajet' 
comes from which ancient text?
Manusmriti.
20041
What is the significance of the $1248$ Shaka date mentioned in the 
text?
It marks the historical decision to formalise the Panji records under 
King Harisimhadeva ($1326$ A.D.).
20042
What happened to the Panji system for the Kshatriya community 
over time?
It gradually became obsolete or lost, leading them to follow the 
practice of avoiding only the immediate 'Dada' and 'Matrika' 
villages.
20043
Which Moola is found in seven different Gotras, including 
Shandilya, Vatsa, and Kashyapa?
Brahmapura.
20044
In a Panji check, the paternal side ancestors of the groom are 
checked for how many generations?
Six ($6$) ancestors (Pitrikula).
20045
In a Panji check, the maternal side ancestors of the groom are 
checked for how many generations?
Five ($5$) ancestors (Matrikula).
20046
What is the 'Asvajana-patra'?
A document proving that the bride and groom are not related by 
prohibited bloodlines.
20047
The 'Genome Mapping' mentioned in the title is a modern 
metaphorical comparison to which traditional practice?
The meticulous genealogical record-keeping of the Mithila Panji.
20048
Who was 'Mahesh Thakur' in the context of Maithil social history?
A Great Scholar-King (Maharaja) of the Khandavala dynasty who 
provided revised legal rulings on marriage prohibitions.
20049
In the list of the $20$ Maithil Brahmin Gotras, which Gotra is listed 
first?
Shandilya.
20050
How does the text define 'Beeji' in the context of lineage?
The primary source or seed ancestor from whom the entire family 
tree is traced.
20051
The 'Tri-pravara' Shandilya Gotra is traced back to Shandilya, Asit, 
and _____.
Deval
20052
Which Gotra corresponds to the sages 'Shakti', 'Vashishtha', and 
'Parashar'?
Parashar Gotra.
20053
Which Gotra corresponds to the sages 'Bharadwaja', 'Angiras', and 
'Barhaspatya'?
Bharadwaja Gotra.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
20054
Why is marriage prohibited between the children of a mother's 
brother and the mother's sister?
They are considered 'Matripaksha' (maternal relatives) within the 
prohibited five-generation degree.
20055
The 'Pancha-Dravida' Brahmin group includes Karnataka, Gurjara, 
Tailanga, Dravida, and _____.
Maharashtriya
20056
Where was the 'Vatsa' Gotra's ancestral knowledge regarding rituals 
codified?
In the Chhandogya and Vajasaneyi Vedic branches.
20057
What is 'Pachhbaripar' status?
The category of Maithil Brahmins who are not in the 'Shrotriya' 
(high-status) lists.
20058
Identify the historical significance of the 'Saka 1019' date in the text. It is associated with King Nanyadeva, the founder of the Karnata 
dynasty in Mithila.
20059
What is the title of the famous encyclopedia written by Jyotirishwar 
Thakur that mentions Mithila's social structure?
Varna Ratnakara.
20060
According to the Panji system, what is the consequence of marrying 
within one's 'Svajana' group?
The offspring are considered 'Chandala' (outcast) and the family 
loses its Vedic status.
20061
During which historical period was the Panji Prabandh established 
for the purpose of caste purity?
The late medieval period.
20062
The Panji Prabandh was primarily created for Brahmins, 
Kshatriyas, and which other social group?
Karna Kayasthas.
20063
What term refers to the introduction of seven generations and the 32 
ancestors required for verifying marriage eligibility in Mithila?
Uddeh.
20064
In the context of Panji, how many maternal and paternal ancestors 
collectively form the required 'Uddeh'?
32
20065
The Maithil Brahmin community is divided into three main 
hierarchical categories: Srotriya, Yogya, and _____.
Panjibadh.
20066
What term is used for Maithil Brahmins who were not included in 
the official Panji records?
Jaiwar.
20067
According to the Panji rules for Srotriyas, how many generations 
must be avoided on the maternal side for a valid marriage?
Five.
20068
According to the Panji rules for Srotriyas, how many generations 
must be avoided on the paternal side for a valid marriage?
Seven.
20069
How many total 'Gotras' are recognised within the Maithil Brahmin 
community according to the Panji records?
20
20070
How many 'Mula Grams' (ancestral villages) were originally 
identified in the Maithil Panji system?
167
20071
Out of the 167 Mulas, how many are considered the 'primary' or 
'main' Mulas?
34 (later 35).
20072
Which historical King of Mithila is credited with formally 
organizing the Panji Prabandh in 1326 AD?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
20073
Who was the first Panjikar (genealogist) appointed by the King for 
Maithil Brahmins?
Gunakar Jha.
20074
Who was the first Panjikar appointed for the Kshatriyas in Mithila?
Vijaydatt.
20075
What was the specific year in the Shaka era when the Panji system 
was formally implemented?
Shake 1248.
20076
Which Veda and Shakha do the 'Vajasaneyi' Maithil Brahmins 
follow?
Yajurveda, Madhyandina Shakha.
20077
Which Veda and Shakha do the 'Chhandogya' Maithil Brahmins 
follow?
Samaveda, Kauthuma Shakha.
20078
The geographical division of Indian Brahmins puts Maithil 
Brahmins under which larger group?
Pancha Gauda.
20079
What is the primary criteria for marriage eligibility regarding 
'Gotras' in the Maithil system?
The bride and groom must not share the same Gotra.
20080
What is the primary criteria for marriage eligibility regarding 
'Pravaras'?
The bride and groom must not share the same Pravara.
20081
Term: Mula
Definition: The root or original ancestral village of a family lineage.
20082
A lineage tracing back to three sage ancestors is known as a _____.
Tri-pravara.
20083
A lineage tracing back to five sage ancestors is known as a _____.
Pancha-pravara.
20084
Which three Gotras are classified as 'Pancha-pravara' in Maithil 
tradition?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharva.
20085
How many 'Siddhanta' (eligibility checkpoints) are calculated to 
verify marriage rights for a girl or boy?
16
20086
What is the name of the document issued by a Panjikar certifying 
that a couple is not related within prohibited degrees?
Siddhanta Patra (or Aswajan-patra).
20087
What specific type of Panji record tracks historical 'flaws' or 
irregularities in a lineage?
Dushan Panji.
20088
Which Maharaja of Darbhanga is associated with the creation of 
'Shakha Panjis' in the 18th century?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
20089
What term is used for the recorded list of ancestors used to prove 
one is not a 'Sapinda' (blood relative)?
Uddeh.
20090
In the Karna Kayastha community, how many Gotras are typically 
recognised?
Only one (Kashyapa).
20091
What was the catalyst for formalising the Panji system, involving a 
Dharmashastra scholar named Harinath Upadhyaya?
He inadvertently married his own close relative due to a lack of 
recorded genealogy.
20092
Which 7th-century philosopher first mentioned 'Samuha Lekhya' 
(family records) as a means to protect caste purity?
Kumarila Bhatta.
20093
The 'Vajasaneyi' wedding ceremony differs from 'Chhandogya' 
because it can take place _____.
At any time, day or night.
20094
The 'Chhandogya' wedding ceremony is unique because the latter 
part of the marriage occurs only after _____.
Sunset, after seeing the star Arundhati.
20095
Which ancestor is considered the 'Biji Purusha' in a Panji record?
The earliest known progenitor or founder of the lineage.
20096
Into how many categories are the main 35 Mulas divided?
Three (Ayanti Srestha, Second Category, and Madhyam Mula).
20097
What is the name of the branch added to the main Mulas as the 35th 
branch?
Phandah Mula's Kharaur branch.
20098
Gotras like Shandilya, Kashyapa, and Parashara are examples of 
which Pravara type?
Tri-pravara.
20099
Name one of the primary duties of a Panjikar according to the Panji 
Prabandh.
Maintaining and updating the genealogical records of births and 
marriages.
20100
What is the name of the Panji that records the migration from one 
village to another?
Patra Panji.
20101
Which specific ancestor is referred to as the 'Pramatamaha'?
The maternal great-grandfather.
20102
Why were families associated with 'Devdasis' or non-caste 
marriages still recorded in the Panji?
To maintain absolute honesty in genealogy and track lineage 
impacts.
20103
The Panji theory suggests that Maithil culture moved in which 
cardinal direction from the Saptasindhu region?
Eastward (Prachyabhimukhi).
20104
According to the source, the Panji records are written in which 
script?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
20105
How many 'Chaurals' (categories) is the Srotriya Panji divided into? Eight.
20106
Which historical mathematician's lineage is reportedly mentioned in 
the Panji records?
Aryabhata.
20107
The Panji records state that Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's teacher, 
Ishwar Puri, belonged to which Maithil Mula?
Kirmahe Phandah.
20108
What mnemonic or rule is given for protecting Panji books from 
damage?
Protect from water, oil, and loose binding ($Jalat\ raksha\ tailat\ 
raksha$).
20109
During which solar month is it recommended to dry and preserve 
Panji manuscripts?
When the Sun is in Leo (Sinha Rashi), typically mid-August to mid-
September.
20110
Term: Pachhwari Paat
Definition: The genealogical records or groups belonging to the 
western/other Maithil Brahmins outside the Srotriya fold.
20111
What is the purpose of the 'Aswajan-patra'?
To certify that the bride and groom are 'non-kin' or not related by 
blood.
20112
How many ancestors of the bride are checked in the 'Uddeh' 
process?
16 (paternal and maternal lineages).
20113
In the Tri-pravara of the 'Shandilya' Gotra, who are the three sages?
Shandilya, Asit, and Deval.
20114
In the Pancha-pravara of the 'Vatsa' Gotra, name any two of the five 
sages.
Aurva and Chyavana (others: Bhargava, Jamadagnya, Apnuvana).
20115
What is the 'Dushan' in a lineage according to Panji terminology?
A genealogical 'taint' or error, such as marriage outside the caste or 
into a prohibited degree.
20116
The source material equates Panji record-keeping with which 
modern scientific field?
Genome Mapping.
20117
Who was the Maharaja of Mithila who presided over the assembly 
when Panji was first organised?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
20118
According to the Panji rules, why can't a Vatsa Gotra person marry 
a Savarna Gotra person?
Because they share the same ancestors (Pancha-pravara) despite 
different names.
20119
In the list of 20 Gotras, which Gotra is associated with the 'Dehadi' 
root?
Jatukarna.
20120
What was the primary purpose of establishing the 'Panji Prabandh' 
system in Mithila?
To maintain the purity of lineage and ensure 'jati shuddhata' for 
Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
20121
During which historical period was the Panji Prabandh formally 
organised?
The late medieval period, specifically the early 14th century (c. 
1326 AD).
20122
According to the Panji system, what does the term 'Udegh' refer to?
The detailed introduction and identification of 32 specific ancestors.
20123
The Panji system mandates that a marriageable individual must be 
separated from a prospective partner by _____ generations on the 
paternal side.
Seven

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
20124
On the maternal side, how many generations of separation are 
required for a valid marriage in the Panji system?
Five
20125
Identify the three main hierarchical categories into which Maithil 
Brahmins were divided under the Panji system.
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddha.
20126
What term is used to describe those who were not registered within 
the Panji system?
Jaibar
20127
In the Panji records, how many 'Moolgrams' (original ancestral 
villages) were initially established?
167
20128
Who is considered the 'Biji Purush' in a Maithil Brahmin lineage?
The earliest known ancestor from whom the lineage or specific 
'Mool' originates.
20129
Name the Karnat dynasty king who is credited with the formal 
implementation of the Panji system in 1326 AD.
Maharaja Harisimhadeva
20130
The formal decree to document lineages was motivated by a 
controversial marriage involving which scholar?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya
20131
What is the specific Shaka era year associated with the 
implementation of the Panji records?
Shaka 1248
20132
Term: Sidhanta Patra
Definition: A certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that a 
bride and groom are not prohibited relatives.
20133
Who was the first Panjikar appointed for the Maithil Brahmins?
Gunakar Jha
20134
Who were the first Panjikars appointed for the Karna Kayasthas and 
the Kshatriyas, respectively?
Shankardatta (Kayasthas) and Vijayadatta (Kshatriyas).
20135
Maithil Brahmins are divided based on the number of Pravaras they 
have into which two categories?
Tri-pravara (three ancestors) and Panch-pravara (five ancestors).
20136
Which three Gotras are classified as 'Panch-pravara' in the Maithil 
tradition?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharadwaja.
20137
What Vedic branch is followed by the Maithil Brahmins known as 
'Chandogya'?
The Kauthuma shakha of the Samaveda.
20138
The Maithil Brahmins following the 'Vajasaneyi' tradition belong to 
which Veda?
The Shukla Yajurveda (Madhyandina shakha).
20139
What is the function of 'Dushan Panji'?
A record of faults or deviations in lineage, such as inter-caste 
marriages or social transgressions.
20140
Which classification of Maithil Brahmins is also known as 
'Pachhwari-paat'?
The non-Shrotriya Brahmins (Yogya and Panjibaddha).
20141
What term is used to describe the Shrotriya Brahmins' social 
ranking records?
Laukika
20142
According to the source, why did Brahmins differentiate into 
regional groups like Maithil, Utkal, and Kanyakubja?
Due to variations in climate, language, diet, and distinct local 
cultural influences over time.
20143
The Maithil Brahmins are part of which larger traditional group of 
five northern Brahmin communities?
Pancha Gauda
20144
What geographic boundary separates the Pancha Gauda from the 
Pancha Dravida Brahmins?
The Vindhyachal mountain range.
20145
Why is the Panji system likened to 'Genome Mapping' in the source 
material?
Because it scientifically tracks biological lineage and genetic 
inheritance through meticulously documented records.
20146
The term 'Mool' in the Panji system primarily identifies an 
individual's _____.
Ancestral village or place of origin.
20147
True or False: Marriage is permitted between members of the Vatsa 
and Savarna Gotras.
False, as they share common ancestors despite having different 
Gotra names.
20148
In the context of Panji documents, what does 'Digital Imaging' refer 
to in the document's preface?
The modern preservation method used to store ancient palm-leaf 
records in digital format.
20149
Under whose reign did the 'Shakha Panji' (branch records) begin to 
differentiate based on social hierarchy?
Maharaja Madhav Singh
20150
What is the primary rule regarding the Gotra of the bride and 
groom?
They must not belong to the same Gotra (Gotra-exogamy).
20151
How many specific ancestors must a Panjikar verify to ensure a 
marriage is 'Asvajana'?
The ancestors of 32 different lineages (Udegh) related to the couple.
20152
Which Gotra includes the most branches (74) in Mithila according 
to the text?
Kashyapa
20153
The source mentions that Maharaja Harisimhadeva's Panji was 
based on a 'scientific' lineage introduction. What was its main 
feature?
It was a pure 'Vamshavali' (genealogy) focused solely on birth and 
lineage verification.
20154
In 'Panch-pravara', who are the five specific rishis associated with 
the Vatsa Gotra?
Aurva, Chyavana, Bhargava, Jamadagnya, and Apnuvana.
20155
According to the source, when is the best time to dry and preserve 
Panji books to prevent damage?
When the sun enters the Leo (Simha) rashi, typically from mid-
August to mid-September.
20156
What three things should a Panji book be protected from, according 
to the traditional Sanskrit shloka?
Water (jala), oil (taila), and loose binding (shithila-bandhana).
20157
The 16 prohibited relations for a bride are collectively known as 
_____.
Chhadisth (Sixths)

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
20158
Concept: Asvajana Patra
Definition: A declaration that the two parties are not 'Svajana' 
(blood relatives) and are eligible to marry.
20159
Identify one example from the text of a lineage 'fault' (Dushan) 
recorded in ancient Panjis.
Marriage into an 'unknown' lineage or a lineage with an 'inter-caste' 
connection (e.g., Devadasis or leather workers).
20160
Why did the author, Gajendra Thakur, compile this text?
To document and preserve the history and mechanics of the Panji 
system from 450 AD to 2009 AD.
20161
In the Maithil Brahmin community, which Veda is associated with 
the 'Shakha' of Madhyandina?
Yajurveda
20162
What is the significant difference in marriage rituals between 
'Chandogya' and 'Vajasaneyi' Brahmins mentioned in the text?
Chandogya marriages are primarily performed in two parts (Purva 
and Uttara), while Vajasaneyi marriages have no such division.
20163
Who was the famous Maithil poet whose lineage is traceable 
through the Panji system for seven generations?
Vidyapati Thakur
20164
The term 'Sapanayikar' or verification of sapinda refers to avoiding 
relatives within how many degrees?
Seven for the father's side and five for the mother's side.
20165
Which ruler of the Karnat dynasty is credited with the formalisation 
of the Maithil Panji Prabandh?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
20166
In what year (AD) was the Mithila Panji system officially organised 
under royal decree?
1326 AD (Shaka 1248).
20167
What is the primary objective of the Panji Prabandh in Mithila?
To ensure lineage purity and prevent marriages within prohibited 
degrees of kinship.
20168
Name the seven most prominent and well-documented Gotras in the 
Maithil Brahmin society.
Shandilya, Vatsa, Kashyap, Savarna, Parashar, Bharadwaj, and 
Katyayan.
20169
In the Panji system, what does the term 'Mula' signify?
The original ancestral village or 'root' of a specific family lineage.
20170
How many distinct 'Mulas' (roots) were recorded in the records of 
the Maithil Brahmins?
167
20171
How many generations must be avoided on the paternal side 
(Pitripaksha) to be eligible for marriage in the Panji system?
Six generations (historically seven).
20172
How many generations must be avoided on the maternal side 
(Matripaksha) for marriage eligibility?
Five generations.
20173
What is the 'Siddhanta Patra' (or Asawarjana Patra) in the context 
of Maithil marriage?
A certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that the bride and 
groom are not related within prohibited degrees.
20174
The Maithil Brahmins who were recorded in the system but did not 
fall into the highest categories were known as _____.
Panjibaddha.
20175
What is the 'Beaji Purush' in a genealogical tree?
The earliest known common ancestor or the founder of a specific 
lineage.
20176
According to the Panji, Maithil Brahmins primarily belong to which 
two Vedas?
Yajurveda and Samaveda.
20177
Followers of the Madhyandina branch of Yajurveda in Mithila are 
known as _____.
Vajasaneyi.
20178
Followers of the Kauthuma branch of Samaveda in Mithila are 
known as _____.
Chhandog.
20179
Which social category represents the highest tier of Maithil 
Brahmins in the Panji hierarchy?
Shrotriya.
20180
Who was the first scholar appointed by Harisimhadeva to manage 
the Brahmin Panji?
Gunakar Jha.
20181
Who was the first registrar appointed for the Kshatriya community's 
Panji?
Vijayadutta.
20182
What is 'Tri-pravara'?
A Gotra associated with three primary sages (Rishis).
20183
What is 'Panch-pravara'?
A Gotra associated with five primary sages (Rishis).
20184
Which three Gotras among the Maithil Brahmins are identified as 
having 'Panch-pravara'?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharve (or Bhargav).
20185
The 'Dushana Panji' is a specific record used to track _____.
Social or genealogical defects/faults identified within a lineage.
20186
What is the function of the 'Sakha Panji' introduced during the reign 
of Maharaja Madhav Singh?
To track branches of families that moved from their original Mula-
Gram to new locations.
20187
The Panji system excludes those who were not registered during the 
initial census; these people are termed _____.
Jaywar.
20188
Under which Maharaja's reign were the 'Shrotriya' and 'Yogya' 
classifications further rigidified?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
20189
What specific scripture do Maithil Brahmins cite as the source for 
marriage rules regarding 'Sapindas'?
Manusmriti (and Yajnavalkya Smriti).
20190
In the Panji terminology, what is a 'Laukika'?
A subdivision or sub-category within the Shrotriya Panji reflecting 
specific lineage status.
20191
Which scholar is credited with modifying the marriage rule from 7 
paternal generations to 6?
M.M. Mahesh Thakur.
20192
The genealogical record of 32 specific ancestors used in the Panji 
system is called the _____.
Yati-Parichay.
20193
True or False: The Panji system was originally applied to Brahmins, 
Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas alike.
True.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
20194
What material was traditionally used to record and preserve the 
Panji manuscripts?
Palm leaves (Tala-patra).
20195
The historical document 'Tantra Varttika' by Kumarila Bhatta 
mentions 'Samuha Lekhya', which refers to _____.
Collective written records used to preserve family lineage and 
quality.
20196
What is the 'Chhavidth' (Chhattha) in the Panji marriage calculation? The 'sixth' position in the lineage of 16 specific ancestors that must 
be checked for overlap.
20197
In the Panji system, how is the 'Beaji' of a branch family identified? By tracking back to the common ancestor who first settled in the 
original Mula-Gram.
20198
Which Gotra does the 'Karna Kayastha' community exclusively 
belong to in Mithila?
Kashyap.
20199
Which specific script is traditionally used to write the Panji records? Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
20200
How often were the Panji records updated historically?
Continuously, as new births and marriages occurred.
20201
If a man and woman share the same Gotra, their marriage is termed 
_____.
Swarjana (prohibited/incestuous within the clan).
20202
Which time of year is traditionally prescribed for drying and 
preserving Panji manuscripts to prevent damage?
When the Sun is in Leo (August/September), due to the intense dry 
heat.
20203
What does the term 'Panch-Gaur' refer to in the context of Indian 
Brahmins?
The five northern Brahmin groups: Utkala, Kanyakubja, Gauda, 
Maithil, and Saraswat.
20204
How does the Samavedi marriage ceremony in Mithila differ from 
the Yajurvedi one regarding the timing of the 'Uttara Vivaha'?
Samavedis perform it after sunset after viewing the Arundhati star.
20205
The phrase 'Jalat Raksha Tailat Raksha' in Panji manuscripts is a 
warning to protect the book from _____.
Water and oil.
20206
The 'Pachhwari Paat' refers to the genealogical records of which 
group?
Non-Shrotriya Maithil Brahmins (Yogya and Panjibaddha).
20207
The 'Pubari Paat' refers to the genealogical records of which group?
The Shrotriya Maithil Brahmins.
20208
What are 'Ayanthi Shrestha' Mulas?
The 'best' or first-tier original ancestral villages (roots) in the Panji 
hierarchy.
20209
According to the source, why did Brahmins in different regions 
eventually form separate sub-castes like 'Maithil' or 'Kanyakubja'?
Due to regional differences in climate, language, diet, and distinct 
local cultures.
20210
Who is 'Ayachi Mishra' (Bhavanath Mishra) in the context of the 
Panji?
A famous scholar from the Sodarpur Mula known for his selfless 
teaching.
20211
How many 'Chhavidth' (lineage paths) must be checked for a girl to 
ensure no paternal overlap with the groom?
16
20212
The 'Gotra Panji' contains a listing of every Gotra and their _____.
Ancient Mulas (roots).
20213
In the Panji, the status of a person can be 'Adhogami' (downward) 
if they _____.
Marry into a family of a lower genealogical tier.
20214
Concept: Swarjana
Definition: A marriage within the same clan or prohibited degree of 
kinship. Example: Marrying a first cousin.
20215
What is the 'Brahmapura' Mula unique for in the Maithil Panji?
It is a Mula found across multiple different Gotras (Shandilya, 
Vatsa, Kashyap, etc.).
20216
Which Gotra is associated with the sages 'Shandilya, Asit, and 
Deval'?
Shandilya.
20217
The record of 'Kulas' (families) that includes their specific qualities 
and flaws is called _____.
Samuha Lekhya.
20218
What major incident led Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya to advocate 
for the Panji system's formalisation?
He unknowingly married a woman who was genealogically 
ineligible (his own cousin's daughter).
20219
What is the 'Pradhan' rule of the Panji Prabandh regarding marriage 
validity?
A marriage is considered 'Ashastriya' (against scriptures) if 
performed without a 'Siddhanta' from a Panjikar.
20220
Which historical figure is credited with formalising the Panji 
system in Mithila?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva of the Karnata dynasty.
20221
In what year (both Shaka and AD) was the Panji system established 
according to the text?
Shaka 1248, which corresponds to 1326 AD.
20222
The Panji system was initially created to manage the lineages of 
which three social groups?
Brahmins, Kayasthas and Kshatriyas.
20223
According to the marriage rules, how many generations must be 
avoided on the father's side?
Seven generations (modified to six in later practice).
20224
According to the marriage rules, how many generations must be 
avoided on the mother's side?
Five generations.
20225
What term is used to describe those whose lineages are officially 
recorded in the Panji system?
Panjibaddh.
20226
Who was the first 'Panjikar' (genealogist) appointed for the Maithel 
Brahmins?
Guna Kar Jha.
20227
How many 'Gotras' (clans) are recognised within the Maithel 
Brahmin community?
Twenty.
20228
Maithel Brahmins predominantly follow which two Vedas?
Yajurveda (Madhyandina branch) and Samaveda (Kauthuma 
branch).

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
20229
What title is given to the followers of the Madhyandina branch of 
the Yajurveda in Mithila?
Vajasaneyi.
20230
What title is given to the followers of the Kauthuma branch of the 
Samaveda in Mithila?
Chhandoga.
20231
How many primary 'Mula-grams' (original villages) were identified 
as core ancestral roots?
34 (later expanded to 35).
20232
Which two Gotras are prohibited from intermarrying because they 
share the same ancestors?
Vatsa and Savarna.
20233
The five regional divisions of Northern Indian Brahmins are 
collectively known as _____.
Panch-Gaur.
20234
What are the five regional divisions of Southern Indian Brahmins 
called?
Panch-Dravida.
20235
Maithel Brahmins belong to which of the 'Panch-Gaur' groups?
The Maithil group.
20236
What is a 'Siddhanta-patra'?
A formal certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that a bride 
and groom are not prohibited relatives.
20237
Which 7th-century philosopher first mentioned the concept of 
'Samuha Lekhya' (collective records)?
Kumarila Bhatta.
20238
What triggered Maharaja Harisimhadeva to formalise the Panji 
system?
The accidental marriage of a high-ranking scholar, Pandit Harinath 
Upadhyay, to a prohibited relative.
20239
The highest social category created within the Panji system during 
the reign of Maharaja Madhav Singh is _____.
Shrotriya.
20240
What are the four hierarchical categories of Maithel Brahmins 
mentioned in the records?
Shrotriya, Yogya, Panjibaddh and Jaibar.
20241
How many 'Chhattha' (six branches/ancestors) are typically 
calculated to verify marriage eligibility?
Sixteen.
20242
Under what astronomical condition are Panji books historically 
supposed to be dried for preservation?
When the Sun is in the Leo (Simha) constellation.
20243
What is the historical significance of the 'Laukika' in the Shrotriya 
Panji?
It refers to the classification of lineages based on social status and 
historical associations.
20244
Which group within the Maithil community is noted for having only 
one Gotra (Kashyapa)?
Karna Kayasthas.
20245
What happened to the Panji system for Kshatriyas over time?
It largely fell into disuse or was lost.
20246
In the context of Panji, what is 'Asvajanapatra'?
A certificate stating that the parties are not related within prohibited 
degrees.
20247
According to the text, why did regional Brahmin cultures diverge?
Differences in climate, language, diet, clothing and geographical 
isolation.
20248
How does the Chhandoga marriage ritual differ from the 
Vajasaneyi ritual regarding the time of day?
Chhandoga marriages involve an 'Uttara Vivaha' performed after 
sunset after seeing the Arundhati star.
20249
Who was the designated genealogist for the Kayastha community 
during Harisimhadeva's reign?
Shankardatta.
20250
Who was the designated genealogist for the Kshatriya community 
during Harisimhadeva's reign?
Vijayadatta.
20251
What is the primary function of a 'Panjikar'?
To act as a legal and religious authority for identifying ancestral 
relationships and approving marriages.
20252
Why is the Panji system compared to modern 'Genome Mapping' in 
the text?
Because it tracks hereditary traits, lineage flaws and bloodlines over 
1,500 years.
20253
Which scholar's 'Smriti' text provides the foundation for the sapinda 
marriage rules used in Panji?
Yajnavalkya (and Manu).
20254
What is the 'Ayanthi' category in the classification of Mulas?
A group of 'best' or first-tier original villages/lineages.
20255
According to the Panji records, how many 'Rajput' clans were 
identified in the Varna Ratnakara?
Seventy-two (with 64 described in detail).
20256
What is the term for a marriage that occurs despite being within 
prohibited degrees?
Svajana marriage.
20257
The 'Siddhanta' ceremony must be performed before which event?
The wedding ceremony.
20258
Which Veda branch includes the 'Vedarmbha' sacrament that the 
Chhandoga branch lacks?
Vajasaneyi (Yajurveda).
20259
How does the Panji track social 'degradation' over time?
Through the recording of 'Dushanas' or specific events that lowered 
a family's status.
20260
Which historical figure is credited with formally initiating the 'Panji 
Prabandha' (genealogical records) in Mithila?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
20261
In what year (Saka era) was the Panji system formally organised 
under royal patronage?
1248 Saka (approximately 1326 AD).
20262
What is the primary purpose of the Mithila 'Panji' system?
To ensure 'Jati Shuddhi' (caste purity) by regulating marriage 
eligibility and preventing incest.
20263
In the context of Panji Prabandha, what does 'Udteh' represent?
An introduction to 32 specific ancestors used to verify marriage 
eligibility.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
20264
According to the Panji rules, how many generations must be 
avoided on the paternal side for a marriage to be valid?
Seven generations (later modified to six for certain groups).
20265
According to the Panji rules, how many generations must be 
avoided on the maternal side for a marriage to be valid?
Five generations.
20266
The Three main social classifications of Maithil Brahmins created 
during the Panji Prabandha are Shrotriya, Yogya, and _____.
Panjibaddha.
20267
What term describes Maithil Brahmins whose ancestors were not 
initially registered in the Panji system but were included later?
Jaybar.
20268
How many total 'Gotras' are recognised among Maithil Brahmins 
according to the source material?
20
20269
Which two Vedic branches represent the primary division of 
Maithil Brahmins based on rituals?
Chhandogya (Samaveda) and Vajasaneyi (Yajurveda).
20270
The original number of 'Muls' (village origins) established in the 
Panji records was _____.
167
20271
Out of the 167 total Muls, how many are considered the 'main' or 
'Mukhya' Muls?
35 (originally 34, with one added later).
20272
What is a 'Biji Purush' in the Panji system?
The earliest known progenitor or 'seed' ancestor of a specific lineage.
20273
What defines 'Tri-pravara' in the context of Gotra identification?
A lineage that identifies three specific ancestral Rishis who 
composed Vedic hymns.
20274
What defines 'Pancha-pravara' in the context of Gotra identification? A lineage that identifies five specific ancestral Rishis who 
composed Vedic hymns.
20275
Name the three Gotras that typically follow the 'Pancha-pravara' 
tradition.
Vatsa, Savarna, and Garga.
20276
What is the 'Siddhanta Patra' (or Aswajan-patra)?
A formal certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that the bride 
and groom are not prohibited relatives.
20277
The five regional groups of North Indian Brahmins (Pancha-Gauda) 
include Utkala, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Saraswata, and _____.
Maithila.
20278
Which scholar's work, 'Tantra Varttika', provides early evidence of 
'Samuha Lekhya' (collective genealogical records)?
Kumarila Bhatta.
20279
In the Panji system, what does the term 'Chhattha' (Sixth) refer to?
The 16 specific ancestral relationship points from which a bride or 
groom must be at least six generations removed.
20280
Why is marriage prohibited between the Vatsa and Savarna Gotras 
despite being distinct names?
They share the same ancestors (progenitors).
20281
Which Brahmin branch performs marriage rituals specifically after 
sunset, following the sighting of the star Arundhati?
Chhandogya (Samavedic) Brahmins.
20282
In the categorization of Muls, which group is known as 'Ayanti 
Shrestha' (the first rank)?
Lineages like Khadaure, Khauade, Budhabade, and Madariye.
20283
How many classifications of Panji books currently exist (e.g., Mul, 
Shakha, Gotra, etc.)?
Seven.
20284
What triggered the formalization of the Panji system by Raja 
Harisimhadeva?
A scholar, Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya, inadvertently married his 
own cousin due to the lack of written records.
20285
The 'Laukika' refers to the specific shakha records primarily 
maintained for which group?
Shrotriya Brahmins.
20286
Concept: Sakit (Prohibited)
Definition: A condition where a marriage is disallowed due to 
overlapping maternal or paternal lineages within prohibited degrees.
20287
How does the Panji record system treat children born from 
prohibited or 'Swajana' marriages?
They were historically categorised as 'Chandala' or socially 
excluded from the Aryan dignity.
20288
What is the significance of 'Mul-Gram' in the Panji system?
It identifies the original ancestral village of a family, distinct from 
their current residence.
20289
Which Gotra includes the most branches (74) identified in Mithila?
Kashyapa.
20290
The record of marriage permissions and lineage updates initiated 
during the reign of Maharaja Madhava Singh (c. 1760 AD) is 
known as _____.
Shakha Panji.
20291
What is the 'Pancha-Dravida' classification of Brahmins?
The five South Indian Brahmin groups residing south of the 
Vindhya mountains.
20292
True or False: The Panji system was historically applied to Maithil 
Brahmins, Karna Kayasthas, and certain Rajput lineages.
True.
20293
Which Panji specifically documents the transition of a person's 
status to a lower grade due to marriage choices?
Paati Panji.
20294
The first official Panjikar appointed for the Karna Kayastha 
community was _____.
Shankardatta.
20295
The first official Panjikar appointed for the Maithil Brahmins was 
_____.
Gunakar Jha.
20296
According to the Panji system, what is the 'Brahmapura' Mul 
exception?
It is a Mul that is found across multiple Gotras like Shandilya, 
Vatsa, and Kashyapa.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
20297
Which historical record details inter-caste or social exceptions, such 
as marriages with Devadasis or unknown lineages?
Dushana Panji (specifically the sections documenting social history).
20298
What is the Vedic 'Sakha' of Samavedic Maithil Brahmins?
Kauthuma.
20299
What is the Vedic 'Sakha' of Yajurvedic Maithil Brahmins?
Madhyandina.
20300
In the Panji system, the term 'Adhogami' describes what social 
phenomenon?
The downward movement of family status (Paati) due to marriages 
into lower-ranked lineages.
20301
What is the meaning of 'Asapinda' in marriage eligibility?
A person who does not share a common 'Pinda' (ancestral 
body/offering) within the prohibited generation limits.
20302
According to the Panji theory, what modern scientific concept 
validates the ancient Rishis' marriage rules?
Genome mapping and the study of chromosomes.
20303
Which Panji classification documents the presence of female names 
in the lineage?
Makaranda-Amaria Panji (within Udteh).
20304
Who was the first official Panjikar for the Rajput (Kshatriya) 
community in Mithila?
Vijayadatta.
20305
How many types of 'Chhattha' (ancestry check-points) are listed to 
determine if a bride is a 'Swajana'?
16
20306
The record of high-ranking families that maintained strict ritual 
purity and were listed in the 'Laukika' is the _____.
Shrotriya Panji.
20307
What is the historical significance of Maharaja Mahesh Thakur in 
the Panji system?
He formalised the rule that 7 generations on the paternal side must 
be excluded, which became widely accepted.
20308
In Panji terminology, what does 'Pachhwari-Paati' refer to?
The social category of Maithil Brahmins who are not in the 
Shrotriya group.
20309
Which Mul-Gram corresponds to the 'Oini' lineage?
Kashyapa Gotra.
20310
The 'Varnaratnakara' documents how many Rajput clans in Mithila? 72 clans (though 64 are primarily described).
20311
What was the role of 'Modanand Jha' in the Panji tradition?
He was a 18th-generation descendant of Gunakar Jha and a 
renowned Panjikar (Panji-Shastra Martanda).
20312
According to the Panji text, what is the relationship between Gotra 
and 'Pita' (Father)?
Gotra is the identification of the ancient patriarch/Rishi of the 
paternal lineage.
20313
Why is the preservation of Tirhuta script considered essential for 
the Panji system?
The vast majority of ancient Panji records are written exclusively in 
this script.
20314
Which Panji serves as a repository for academic titles and 
educational achievements of ancestors since 450 AD?
Prachin Panji (Ancient Records).
20315
True or False: The Panji system prohibits marriage with the 
offspring of the mother's brother (maternal cousin).
True.
20316
What was the primary purpose of the 'Panji Prabandh' system 
established in Mithila?
To maintain caste purity and ensure genealogical correctness for 
marriages among Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
20317
Which ruler is credited with the formalisation and promotion of the 
Panji system in 1326 AD (Shaka 1248)?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva of the Karnata dynasty.
20318
Term: Utegh
Definition: The detailed introduction of 32 ancestral lineages 
required to verify a person's genealogical purity for marriage.
20319
What is the total number of primary 'Gotras' (lineages) identified 
within the Maithil Brahmin community?
Twenty (20).
20320
In the context of the Panji system, what does the term 'Biji Purush' 
signify?
The earliest known founding ancestor or progenitor of a specific 
lineage.
20321
What is a 'Siddhanta Patra' (also known as Aswajanapatra)?
A formal certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that the bride 
and groom are not prohibited relations.
20322
The professional genealogists responsible for maintaining records 
and verifying marriage eligibility are known as _____.
Panjikars
20323
What is the difference between 'Tri-pravara' and 'Panch-pravara'?
Tri-pravara indicates three ancestral sages who composed Vedic 
hymns, while Panch-pravara indicates five such sages.
20324
Which specific Maithil Brahmin category was established for those 
whose lineages were systematically recorded and verified in the 
Panji?
Panjibaddha
20325
Identify the three main hierarchical tiers of Maithil Brahmins 
created under the Panji system.
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddha.
20326
What happened to Brahmins who were not recorded in the official 
Panji system?
They were categorised as 'Jaywar'.
20327
The Panji system identifies how many 'Moola Grams' (original 
villages) from which the 167 lineages spread?
One hundred and sixty-seven (167).
20328
How many main 'Moolas' were initially prioritised from the total 
167?
Thirty-four (34), which later became 35 with the inclusion of the 
Khandam branch.
20329
According to the source, why did Brahmins in India eventually 
divide into regional groups like 'Pancha Gauda' and 'Pancha 
Dravida'?
Due to geographical variations in climate, language, diet, and local 
cultural influences over time.
20330
Which five Brahmin groups constitute the 'Pancha Gauda' 
(Northern group)?
Utkala, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, and Saraswat.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
20331
Which geographical feature serves as the boundary between 'Pancha 
Gauda' and 'Pancha Dravida' Brahmins?
The Vindhyachal mountain range.
20332
Followers of the Madhyandina branch of Yajurveda in Mithila are 
called _____.
Vajasaneyi
20333
Followers of the Kauthuma branch of Samaveda in Mithila are 
called _____.
Chandoga
20334
What is the major procedural difference between Chandoga and 
Vajasaneyi marriage rituals regarding timing?
Chandoga marriages have a specific 'Uttar Vivah' performed after 
sunset following the sighting of the Dhruva star, whereas 
Vajasaneyi rituals have no such timing restriction.
20335
The seventh-century work 'Tantra Varttika', which mentions 
'Samuha Lekhya' (family records), was written by _____.
Kumarila Bhatta
20336
Who was the scholar appointed by Harisimhadeva to organise the 
Panji for Maithil Brahmins?
Gunakar Jha
20337
Which two ancestors are shared between the Savarna and Vatsa 
gotras, preventing marriage between the two lineages?
Aurva and Chyavana (among others, indicating a common origin).
20338
The 'Chhatth' calculation involves searching for common ancestors 
across how many specific points of relationship?
Sixteen (16).
20339
In the Shrotriya category, how many 'Chaupals' or hierarchical 
grades are identified?
Eight (8).
20340
How many total 'Panjis' (books/records) are mentioned for the 
Shrotriya and Yogya/Panjibaddha groups?
185 (32 for Shrotriya and 153 for Yogya/Panjibaddha).
20341
What is 'Sakha Panji'?
A detailed record of the branches and sub-branches of a family 
lineage, tracking the expansion from the original Moola.
20342
The first 'Chhatth' point in marriage eligibility checks refers to 
which ancestor of the bride?
The father of the bride's great-great-grandfather (Vriddha 
Prapitamaha).
20343
Under what condition is a marriage prohibited even if the 
generation counts (5 or 6) are satisfied?
If the bride and groom share the same Gotra or the same Pravara.
20344
In the Panji system, what is the significance of the month from mid-
August to mid-September (Sun in Leo)?
It is the time prescribed for drying and preserving the Panji 
manuscripts using intense sunlight.
20345
The script traditionally used to maintain Panji records is _____.
Tirhuta (Mithilakshara)
20346
Which scholar is noted in the source for achieving first rank in the 
'Panji Shastra Martand' examination conducted by Maharaja 
Kameshwar Singh?
Modanand Jha
20347
How does the Panji system account for families that migrated to 
other regions like Bengal (e.g., Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's ancestors)?
Their origins are traced back to their original Mithila Moola (e.g., 
Khauwal Moola for Chaitanya).
20348
The term used for a girl from an unknown lineage or one whose 
mother's family is unrecorded is _____.
Agnyata (Unknown)
20349
Why is the marriage between Savarna Gotra and Vatsa Gotra 
forbidden?
They share common ancestors (Aurva, Chyavana, Bhargava, 
Jamadagnya, and Apnuvana) making them 'prohibited' relatives.
20350
According to the source, what is the role of the mother's brother's 
progeny in marriage rules?
A groom cannot marry the daughter of his maternal uncle (direct 
offspring of his mother's brother).
20351
The phrase 'Jalat Rakshet Tailat Rakshet' found in Panji 
manuscripts is an instruction to protect the book from what?
Water and Oil.
20352
Who was the scholar appointed to manage the Panji of the Karna 
Kayastha community?
Shankar Datta
20353
Who was the scholar appointed to manage the Panji of the 
Kshatriya community?
Vijay Datta
20354
The source compares the traditional Panji system to which modern 
scientific field?
Genome Mapping (due to its systematic tracking of hereditary traits 
and lineages).
20355
What is the 'Bijivara' or 'Bij' in the Panji system?
The inherent quality or status of a lineage, often subject to change 
based on marriage choices (Adhogami or descending status).
20356
What does 'Pachhwari Paat' refer to in the Maithil Brahmin social 
structure?
The broad group of Brahmins excluding the Shrotriya category.
20357
What is 'Pubari Paat'?
A term sometimes used to refer specifically to the Shrotriya group.
20358
In the ritual context of 'Pravara', what was the original purpose of 
invoking these ancestors during a sacrifice?
To invite Agni (the sacrificial fire) by reciting the names of 
distinguished sage-ancestors.
20359
How does the Panji system treat marriages that occurred with 
families from the 'West' (Paschim Desh)?
They are recorded as marriages into 'Agnyata Kula' (Unknown 
lineages) if the records do not align with the Panji.
20360
Term: Up-Tip
Definition: Supplementary genealogical notes used to update and 
track sub-branches of the Panji records.
20361
Which king's reign saw the introduction of 'Sakha Panji' to separate 
lineages based on social status changes?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
20362
What is the 'Adhogami' nature of the Panji status?
The concept that a family's rank within the Shrotriya/Yogya system 
can decrease if they marry into lower-ranked lineages.
20363
The 'Tantra Varttika' suggests that 'Samuha Lekhya' were 
maintained specifically to avoid forgetting ancestors of which two 
castes?
Kshatriyas and Brahmins.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
20364
What was the significance of the village 'Mandu' for the Kashyapa 
Gotra?
It is the central Moola (ancestral root) for seventy-four different 
branches of that Gotra.
20365
What happens if a Panjikar discovers a 'Dushana' (fault) in a 
lineage during verification?
The marriage eligibility of the family might be compromised or 
their social ranking lowered.
20366
The term used for a female ancestor from a family of temple 
dancers or specific service groups is _____.
Devadasi
20367
How many ancestors are included in the 'Utegh' list to ensure no 
common ancestor is shared within prohibited degrees?
Thirty-two (32).
20368
Which ancestral line does the 16th 'Chhatth' point verify?
The maternal great-grandfather of the bride's maternal grandmother 
(Matri-matamahi-matamaha).
20369
The scholar Vidyapati Thakur belonged to which Moola?
Bisaphi (under the Kashyapa Gotra).
20370
According to the source, why did the Kshatriya Panji system 
eventually fall into disuse?
Primarily due to the passage of time and the loss of systematic 
institutional maintenance.
20371
Identify the primary source material used for writing Panji records 
before the widespread use of paper.
Tala-patra (Palm leaves).
20372
Which ancestral sages are common to the Shandilya gotra?
Shandilya, Asita, and Devala.
20373
Which ancestral sages are common to the Bharadwaja gotra?
Bharadwaja, Angirasa, and Barhaspatya.
20374
The scholar Mahesh Thakur, who founded the Darbhanga Raj, 
belonged to which Moola?
Kharaure (Saurath).
20375
In the context of Panji, what does the term 'Utegh' or 'Udegh' 
represent?
The introduction of 32 ancestral lineages required to verify a 
person's caste purity.
20376
According to the Panji system, how many generations must be 
avoided on the father's side for a valid marriage?
Seven generations.
20377
According to the Panji system, how many generations must be 
avoided on the mother's side for a valid marriage?
Five generations.
20378
Which historical King of Mithila is credited with formalising the 
Panji system in the $14$th century?
King Harisimhadeva.
20379
In what year (AD) was the decision made to formalise Panji records 
to prevent prohibited marriages?
$1326$ AD.
20380
Who was the first 'Panjikar' (registrar) appointed for the Maithil 
Brahmins?
Gunakar Jha.
20381
What specific incident involving Pandit Hari Nath Upadhyay led to 
the formalisation of Panji records?
He unknowingly married his cousin's daughter due to a lack of 
genealogical knowledge.
20382
Maithil Brahmins are divided into which two main Vedic branches 
based on their ritual practices?
Chhandoga (Samaveda) and Vajasaneyi (Yajurveda).
20383
The highest social tier in the Maithil Brahmin hierarchy established 
by the Panji is known as _____.
Shrotriya
20384
What are the four tiers of Maithil Brahmins defined by the Panji 
system?
Shrotriya, Yogya, Panjibaddh, and Jaywar.
20385
How many 'Mula Grams' (original villages) were identified during 
the initial Panji collection?
$167$.
20386
What defines a 'Biji Purush' in the Panji records?
The earliest known ancestor from whom a specific lineage or 'Mula' 
originates.
20387
In Panji terminology, what does 'Siddhanta Patra' (or 'Aswajan-
patra') signify?
A certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that the bride and 
groom are not related within prohibited degrees.
20388
Lineages that are not of the same Gotra but share the same 
ancestors and cannot intermarry are called _____.
Sargotra
20389
Which Gotra includes the pravaras: Sandilya, Asita, and Devala?
Sandilya.
20390
The 'Vatsa' Gotra belongs to which pravara category based on the 
number of ancestors invoked?
Panch-Pravara (Five Pravaras).
20391
Which three Gotras are classified as 'Panch-Pravara' in the Maithil 
system?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bhargava.
20392
How does the marriage of a 'Vajasaneyi' differ from a 'Chhandoga' 
regarding the timing of the 'Uttara Vivah'?
Chhandogas perform it after sunset after seeing the Arundhati star, 
whereas Vajasaneyis have no such time restriction.
20393
The rule stating a person cannot marry someone from their own 
Gotra is known as _____.
Sagotra Vivah Nishedh
20394
What are the six essential prohibited generational positions checked 
in the 'Chhadwa' rule?
The 6th position from 16 specific maternal and paternal ancestral 
lines.
20395
In the Panji system, the term 'Mula' refers to the _____.
Original ancestral village or lineage.
20396
Which Gotra consists of the lineages: Kashyapa, Avatsara, and 
Naidhruva?
Kashyapa.
20397
In the context of social status, 'Pachhwaripath' refers to Brahmins 
who are _____.
Not of the Shrotriya category (those who fall outside the highest 
tier).
20398
According to the Panji text, what happens to the children of a 
marriage that violates 'Svajana' (prohibited relation) rules?
They were historically considered 'Chandal' or socially boycotted 
from Aryan dignity.
20399
The term used for the written genealogical registers that track 
lineage quality and flaws is _____.
Samuh Lekhya

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
20400
Which philosopher mentioned 'Samuh Lekhya' in his work 'Tantra 
Vartika' to highlight the preservation of lineage?
Kumarila Bhatta.
20401
How did King Madhav Singh influence the Panji system in the 
$18$th century?
He initiated the 'Shakha Panji' and formalised the tiered 
classification of Shrotriya.
20402
In Karna Kayasthas, how many Gotras are traditionally recognised?
One (Kashyapa).
20403
What are the 'Pancha Gauda' Brahmin divisions mentioned in the 
text?
Utkala, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, and Saraswat.
20404
Which geographical feature divides the 'Pancha Gauda' from the 
'Pancha Dravida' Brahmins?
The Vindhyachal Mountains.
20405
Lineages belonging to the 'Ayan-Shrestha' (First Category) of the 35 
main mulas include _____.
Khadaure, Khauade, Budhabade, and Sodarpur.
20406
What is the 'Utegh Panji' specifically used for?
Identifying 'Sapinda' relationships by tracing six generations of 
ancestors.
20407
Concept: Pan Panji
Definition: A register that records the lineage and the specific 
original village (Mulagram) of ancestors.
20408
The first 'Chhadwa' position for a bride is her _____.
Paternal great-grandfather's paternal grandfather.
20409
The ninth 'Chhadwa' position for a bride is her _____.
Maternal grandfather's paternal great-grandfather.
20410
Why is marriage prohibited between the 'Savarna' and 'Vatsa' 
Gotras?
They share the same ancestors (Aurva, Chyavana, etc.), making 
them 'Sargotra'.
20411
The 'Dushan' or lineage flaw of 'Rajaka' refers to an ancestor 
marrying into a _____ family.
Washerman's
20412
The 'Dushan' of 'Charmakari' refers to an ancestor marrying into a 
_____ family.
Cobbler's
20413
Lineages like 'Khadaure', 'Madaie', and 'Darihare' belong to which 
ranking of Mulas?
Ayan Shrestha (First Tier).
20414
Lineages like 'Dighwe', 'Belanche', and 'Panchobhe' belong to which 
ranking of Mulas?
Madhyam (Middle Tier).
20415
Which Brahmin branch performs the 'Vedarambh' ceremony as one 
of their four primary sacraments?
Vajasaneyi (Yajurveda branch).
20416
The person from whom a lineage spreads into different villages 
while maintaining the same root is called the _____.
Biji Purush
20417
What rule did Mahamahopadhyaya Mahesh Thakur establish 
regarding paternal lineage marriage?
He deemed the 7th generation acceptable for marriage (as opposed 
to 8th).
20418
Which month of the year is traditionally recommended for drying 
Panji books to protect them from insects?
When the Sun is in Leo (approx. $16$ August to $15$ September).
20419
What is the meaning of 'Asapinda' in the context of marriage 
selection?
One who is not a 'Sapinda' (blood relative within restricted degrees) 
to the mother or father.
20420
The historical figure 'Lakhima' in the Panji records is associated 
with which Gotra and lineage?
Kashyapa Gotra, Sakaradi Mula.
20421
The term 'Laukika' in the Shrotriya community refers to _____.
The specific lineage rankings or categories within the Shrotriya 
Panji.
20422
How many 'Chhadwa' (prohibited ancestral positions) must be 
checked for both the bride and groom?
$16$ positions each (total of $32$).
20423
Which Gotra is associated with the Pravara 'Shakti, Vashistha, and 
Parashara'?
Parashara.
20424
Which Gotra is associated with the Pravara 'Angirasa, Vashistha, 
and Barhaspatya'?
Gautama.
20425
In the Panji, the 'Dushan' resulting from marriage into the family of 
a temple dancer is called _____.
Devdasi Dushan
20426
Which registrar was appointed for the Kshatriya community 
lineages?
Vijayadatta.
20427
Which registrar was appointed for the Karna Kayastha community 
lineages?
Shankardatta.
20428
The practice of 'Aruandhati Darshan' during marriage is specific to 
which group of Brahmins?
Chhandoga (Samaveda branch).
20429
The Panji system records historical titles; which lineage is 
associated with the title 'Mahamahopadhyaya'?
Various learned lineages including Sodarpur, Mandar, and Khauade.
20430
What does the term 'Jaywar' signify in the modern context of the 
Panji records?
Lineages whose detailed genealogical records are currently 
unavailable or incomplete.
20431
The rule for excluding relatives for marriage based on common 
ancestors is called the _____ rule.
Sapinda
20432
According to the Panji records, the lineage of the famous poet 
Vidyapati is traceable for at least _____ generations.
$7$ generations prior to him.
20433
The manual verification process where a Panjikar checks ancestors 
before a wedding is called _____.
Adhikar Janch (Authority Check)
20434
What is the 'Dushan' of 'Yavani'?
A lineage flaw resulting from marriage with a person of non-Hindu 
(Yavana/Muslim) background.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
20435
Why is the Panji system compared to 'Genome Mapping' in the 
text's title?
Because it tracks hereditary traits, lineage, and genetic exclusivity 
across centuries.
20436
The 'Vishnuvriddha' Gotra features which specific Pravaras?
Vishnuvriddha, Kaunipuchha, and Trasadasyu.
20437
Which lineage is described as having $74$ branches originating 
from the Mandar village?
Kashyapa Gotra, Mandar Mula.
20438
The 'Pancha Dravida' Brahmin group includes which five regional 
communities?
Gurjara, Tailanga, Dravida, Maharashtra, and Karnata.
20439
Who provided the $16$th Chhadwa rule for the maternal 
grandmother's maternal grandmother's line?
The Panji tradition following the 'Chhadwa' algorithm.
20440
The Panji records for the Shrotriya community are divided into 
eight categories called _____.
Chaurals
20441
In Panji books, what does the mnemonic 'Jalat Raksha, Tailat 
Raksha' advise?
Protect the manuscript from water and oil.
20442
Which Gotra includes the Pravara 'Shandilya, Asita, and Devala'?
Shandilya.
20443
How did the Panji system respond to historical marriages into non-
Maithil or lower caste groups?
By recording them as 'Dushan' (flaws) to alert future generations 
during marriage negotiations.
20444
Who is credited with the formal initiation of the 'Panji Prabandh' 
genealogical system in Mithila in 1326 AD?
King Harisimhadeva of the Karnata dynasty.
20445
The Panji Prabandh was established to maintain the lineage purity 
of which three social groups?
Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
20446
According to the marriage laws of Panji Prabandh, a man is 
prohibited from marrying a woman within how many generations of 
his paternal lineage?
Seven generations.
20447
What is the status of Maithil Brahmins who are not formally 
recorded in the Panji registers?
Jaywar.
20448
Maithil Brahmins are divided into how many distinct 'Gotras' 
(clans)?
20
20449
In Maithil genealogy, what does the term 'Mula' designate?
The ancestral village or place of origin for a specific family lineage.
20450
How many original 'Mulas' (ancestral villages) were initially 
identified for Maithil Brahmins?
167
20451
What defines a 'Tri-Pravara' lineage in Maithil tradition?
A lineage whose three ancient ancestors were composers of 
Rigvedic hymns.
20452
Which specific Gotras are classified as 'Pancha-Pravara' (having 
five Vedic hymn-composing ancestors)?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharv.
20453
Who was the first 'Panjikar' (genealogist) appointed by King 
Harisimhadeva for the Brahmin community?
Gunakar Jha.
20454
Which Panjikar was initially appointed to manage the genealogical 
records for the Karna Kayasthas?
Shankardatta.
20455
What is a 'Siddhanta Patra' (or Siddhanta)?
A formal certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming a couple's legal 
and genealogical right to marry.
20456
Maithil Brahmins primarily belong to which two Vedic schools?
Yajurveda (Madhyandina branch) and Samaveda (Kauthuma 
branch).
20457
Yajurvedic Maithil Brahmins of the Madhyandina branch are 
known by what title?
Vajasaneyi.
20458
Samavedic Maithil Brahmins of the Kauthuma branch are known 
by what title?
Chhandoga.
20459
The 'Vindhya' mountains historically serve as the boundary between 
the Pancha Gauda and the _____.
Pancha Dravida.
20460
Under which group of the 'Pancha Gauda' classification do Maithil 
Brahmins fall?
Maithil.
20461
In the context of the Panji system, what is the 'Biji Purush'?
The earliest known progenitor or founder of a specific lineage or 
'Mula'.
20462
What was the historical catalyst for King Harisimhadeva to 
formalise the Panji system in the 14th century?
A high-ranking scholar inadvertently marrying a close relative due 
to a loss of genealogical knowledge.
20463
Which philosopher's 7th-century work, the 'Tantra Varttika', first 
mentions 'Sammuha Lekhya' (collective lineage records)?
Kumarila Bhatta.
20464
The highest category in the Maithil Brahmin social hierarchy, 
strictly adhering to Panji rules, is called _____.
Shrotriya.
20465
The supplementary genealogical records used for tracking specific 
family branches are known as _____.
Shakha Panji.
20466
Which King ordered the creation of 'Shakha Panjis' (branch 
records) to further distinguish family rankings?
Maharaja Madhava Singh.
20467
In the calculation of marriage eligibility, how many specific 
relationship nodes, known as 'Chhatth', are tracked?
16
20468
If a shared ancestor is found within the paternal line of both the 
bride and groom at the 'Chhatth' level, the marriage is _____.
Forbidden.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
20469
An overlap in the 'Chhatth' nodes is only genealogically permissible 
if it occurs within the _____.
Maternal lineage of the groom.
20470
The great-great-grandfather of the bride is historically identified as 
the _____.
First Chhatth (Prathama Chhatth).
20471
Which script was traditionally used to write and preserve the 
Maithil Panji records?
Tirhuta (also known as Mithilakshar).
20472
Maithil Brahmins following the Samavedic tradition perform the 
'Uttara Vivaha' (second part of the wedding) only after observing 
which star?
Arundhati.
20473
The 'Laukika' refers specifically to the lineage records and status of 
which group?
Shrotriya Brahmins.
20474
Which Gotra is associated with the 'Mandane' Mula, spanning 74 
branches in Mithila?
Kashyap.
20475
What term describes the downward shift in family status resulting 
from marriage into a lower-ranked lineage?
Adho-gami.
20476
According to the 'Dushana Panji', why were certain lineages given 
the title 'Taskar' (Thief)?
The genealogists added the title after a man abducted and married a 
woman to protect the Panji's integrity.
20477
How does the Panji system view marriage with the daughter of a 
maternal uncle?
It is strictly forbidden as a violation of genealogical purity.
20478
In the Panji system, what is the 'Pachhwari-paat'?
A collective term for the Brahmin classes ranked below the 
Shrotriya and Yogya divisions.
20479
What is the literal meaning of 'Panji Prabandh'?
The management or arrangement of genealogical registers.
20480
The ancestor who lived circa 400 AD and is considered the Biji 
Purush of the Mandan Mula is _____.
Trivenir Bhatta.
20481
Which Mula group, including Kharaure and Khauware, occupies 
the 'Ayan-Shreshtha' (Foremost Excellent) rank?
The first category of the 35 primary Mulas.
20482
What is the 'Mula-Gram'?
The specific village identified as the primary residence of the 
progenitor of a clan.
20483
How are Panji books traditionally preserved to prevent damage 
from moisture and pests?
They are dried in the sun when it enters the sign of Leo (Sinha 
Rashi).
20484
The term 'Sapinda' refers to relatives sharing a common _____.
Ancestral offering or body (Pinda).
20485
Which class of Brahmins is identified as 'Yogya'?
The second highest rank in the Maithil social order, below Shrotriya 
but above Panjibaddh.
20486
What scriptural rule is the foundation for the 5 and 7 generation 
marriage restrictions in Mithila?
Matritah Panchami, Pitritah Saptami (from the Manu Smriti).
20487
In the Panji system, what is the significance of 'Brahmapura'?
It is a rare Mula found across multiple Gotras, such as Shandilya, 
Vatsa, and Kashyap.
20488
What does 'Genome Mapping' signify in the title of the provided 
text?
A modern metaphorical comparison to the ancient, precise 
genealogical tracking of the Panji system.
20489
Why is marriage prohibited for someone designated as 'Svajana'?
They are considered too closely related, and offspring of such 
unions were historically likened to 'Chandalas'.
20490
Which ruler is credited with institutionalising the Panji system in 
Mithila?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva of the Karnat dynasty.
20491
In what year (Shaka and AD) was the Panji Prabandh formally 
established?
Shaka 1248, which corresponds to 1326 AD.
20492
According to the Panji system, how many generations must be 
avoided for marriage on the paternal side?
Six or seven generations.
20493
The earliest reference to written lineage records ('Samuha Lekhya') 
is found in which 7th-century text?
The 'Tantra Vartika' by Kumarila Bhatta.
20494
What term refers to the earliest known ancestor of a specific lineage 
in the Panji system?
Beeji Purush.
20495
What does the term 'Mulgram' signify in Maithil genealogy?
The original or ancestral village of a family's lineage.
20496
Into which two major Vedic branches are Maithil Brahmins 
primarily divided?
Samavedi (Chhandogya) and Shukla Yajurvedi (Vajasaneyi).
20497
What is the difference in marriage timing between the Chhandogya 
and Vajasaneyi branches?
Chhandogya marriages occur in two parts (East and West), while 
Vajasaneyi marriages have no such division.
20498
Define 'Tri-pravara' in the context of Maithil Gotras.
A Gotra whose ancestors composed three specific hymns or 
sections of the Rigveda.
20499
Define 'Panch-pravara' in the context of Maithil Gotras.
A Gotra whose ancestors composed five specific hymns or sections 
of the Rigveda.
20500
Which three Gotras are classified as Panch-pravara?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Garga.
20501
Who were the three sages associated with the Shandilya Gotra?
Shandilya, Asit, and Deval.
20502
List the three sages (Pravaras) associated with the Kashyap Gotra.
Kashyap, Avatsara, and Naidhruva.
20503
Who was the scholar appointed by Harisimhadeva to manage 
Brahmin Panji records?
Gunakar Jha of the Pandua Mul.
20504
Who was appointed to manage the Panji records for the Karna 
Kayasthas?
Shankardatt.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
20505
Who was the first registrar appointed for the Kshatriya Panji 
records?
Vijaydatt.
20506
What are the four categories of Maithil Brahmins created by the 
Panji system?
Shrotriya, Yogya, Panjibaddh, and Jaywar.
20507
What is the 'Siddhanta Patra' (or 'Asvajan Patra')?
A certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that a couple is not 
related within prohibited degrees.
20508
What information is contained in a family's 'Utivedh'?
A complete ancestral introduction covering 32 distinct maternal and 
paternal lineage points.
20509
How many 'Muls' (root lineages) were originally identified for 
Maithil Brahmins?
167
20510
Out of the 167 original Muls, how many were designated as 
'Mukhya' (main) Muls?
34 (later expanded to 35).
20511
The 'Panch Gaur' Brahmin group includes Maithils, Saraswats, 
Kanyakubjas, Gaurs, and _____.
Utkals.
20512
Which geographical feature divides the 'Panch Gaur' from the 
'Panch Dravid' Brahmins?
The Vindhyachal mountain range.
20513
What is the status of a child born from a marriage between 
prohibited relatives (Svajana)?
The child was historically considered 'Chandala' and excluded from 
Aryan social rites.
20514
Term: Chhad-vith
Definition: The 16 specific ancestral lines checked to ensure 
marriage eligibility.
20515
Who is the primary Chhad-vith checked for a bride's eligibility?
The father of the bride's great-great-grandfather.
20516
Which Gotra is the only one found among Karna Kayasthas?
Kashyap.
20517
Which type of Panji record tracks family defects or social lapses?
Dushan Panji.
20518
What is the function of the 'Shakha Panji'?
It provides a detailed branch-wise genealogy including titles, 
education, and migrations.
20519
Who introduced the 'Shakha Panji' during his reign in the 18th 
century?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
20520
What script is traditionally used to write Panji records?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
20521
Which Brahmin category is also known as 'Pubaripar'?
Shrotriya.
20522
Which Brahmin category is also known as 'Pachhbaripar'?
The remaining categories excluding Shrotriyas (Yogya, Panjibaddh, 
Jaywar).
20523
How many 'Chouvala' (sub-categories) exist within the Shrotriya 
Panji?
Eight.
20524
What happens to the rank of a Shrotriya lineage if they marry into a 
lower category?
The lineage rank descends (Adhogami) and may eventually lose 
Shrotriya status.
20525
What is the 'Asvajan Patra' specifically intended to prove?
That the bride and groom are not 'Svajana' (relatives within 
prohibited degrees).
20526
How does a 'Panjikar' traditionally preserve Panji books from 
damage?
By protecting them from water, oil, and loose binding, and drying 
them in the sun during the month of Bhadra.
20527
In the context of 'Chhad-vith', the 10th check refers to whom?
The maternal grandfather of the bride's paternal great-grandmother.
20528
Explain the significance of the 7th Chhad-vith.
It tracks the paternal great-grandfather of the bride's paternal 
grandmother.
20529
Why did the Panji system decline among the Kshatriya (Rajput) 
community in Mithila?
Due to the loss of written records and shifts in social practices over 
time.
20530
What major legal/religious texts define the marriage rules used in 
the Panji system?
The Manusmriti and Yajnavalkya Smriti.
20531
How many sages are mentioned in the Pravara for the Parashar 
Gotra?
Three (Shakti, Vashistha, and Parashar).
20532
Which Gotra is associated with the sages Angira, Vashistha, and 
Barhaspatya?
Gautam.
20533
The 'Laukik' system is primarily associated with which Brahmin 
rank?
Shrotriya.
20534
What was the historical consequence of a 'Panjikar' issuing a false 
eligibility certificate?
They were given titles like 'Taskar' (thief) as a mark of disgrace.
20535
Which Panji type specifically details the 'Sapinda' relationship to six 
generations?
Utivedh Panji.
20536
What is the 'Ayanthi Shrestha' category in the hierarchy of Muls?
The first or highest grade of ancestral roots, including Muls like 
Kharaure and Khauare.
20537
What determines if a 'Chhad-vith' relationship is permissible for 
marriage?
A Chhad-vith ancestor is prohibited if found in the groom's paternal 
line, but permissible if found in the groom's maternal line.
20538
Which Gotra is associated with the sages Katyayan, Vishnu, and 
Angira?
Katyayan.
20539
Which Gotra includes the sages Vishnuvriddha, Paurukutsa, and 
Trasadasyu?
Vishnuvriddha.
20540
Name the sages in the Bharadwaj Gotra's Pravara.
Bharadwaj, Angira, and Barhaspatya.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
20541
What is the 16th Chhad-vith check?
The maternal grandfather of the bride's maternal grandmother's 
maternal grandmother.
20542
In the context of Mithila, what is the 'Panji Prabandh'?
A comprehensive system of genealogical records used to maintain 
caste purity and manage genetic mapping.
20543
Which historical ruler is credited with the formal initiation of the 
Panji Prabandh in Mithila?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
20544
According to the source material, in which year (Shaka and AD) 
was the Panji Prabandh decision formalised?
Shaka 1248, which corresponds to 1326 AD.
20545
Who was appointed to manage the Panji records for the Maithil 
Brahmins?
Gunakar Jha.
20546
Who was the designated Panjikar for the Karna Kayasthas during 
Harisimhadeva's reign?
Shankardutt.
20547
Who was the primary record keeper appointed for the Kshatriyas in 
the Panji system?
Vijaydutt.
20548
What is the primary purpose of 'Genome Mapping' (Genome 
Mapping) mentioned in the Maithil Panji context?
To ensure the purity of lineage and prevent marriage within 
prohibited degrees of kinship.
20549
The Panji system originally covered three social groups: Brahmins, 
Kayasthas, and _____.
Kshatriyas.
20550
What is the term for the 32-generation lineage introduction required 
for marriage eligibility in the Panji system?
Utegh.
20551
How many total 'Gotras' are recognised among Maithil Brahmins 
according to the text?
20
20552
How many 'Gotras' are considered well-established and numerically 
dominant in Maithil society?
7
20553
What is a 'Moola' in the Panji system?
The original village or root settlement of a specific lineage.
20554
How many 'Moola-grams' (original villages) are recorded in the 
Maithil Brahmin Panji?
167
20555
What is the 'Pravara' of a Gotra?
The list of distinguished sage ancestors who composed Vedic 
hymns, usually numbering three or five.
20556
How does a 'Tri-pravara' differ from a 'Panch-pravara'?
A Tri-pravara has three distinguished sage ancestors, while a Panch-
pravara has five.
20557
The Gotras Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharadwaj are classified as having 
_____ pravaras.
Five.
20558
In the Panji system, what is 'Sapinda'?
The state of sharing the same body particles/bloodline within a 
specified number of generations.
20559
For marriage eligibility, how many generations must typically be 
avoided on the father's side?
Seven generations (or six in some revised interpretations).
20560
For marriage eligibility, how many generations must be avoided on 
the mother's side?
Five generations.
20561
What is a 'Siddhanta' (or Ashvajana-patra) in the Panji tradition?
A formal certificate of eligibility issued by a Panjikar confirming 
that the bride and groom are not prohibited relatives.
20562
Which official is authorised by the state to verify genealogical 
records and issue marriage certificates?
The Panjikar.
20563
Maithil Brahmins are followers of which two Vedas?
Yajurveda and Samaveda.
20564
Yajurvedic Maithil Brahmins following the Madhyandina branch 
are known as _____.
Vajasaneyi.
20565
Samavedic Maithil Brahmins following the Kauthuma branch are 
known as _____.
Chhandogya.
20566
Which legendary Maithil poetâ€™s lineage is extensively tracked in 
the Panji records?
Vidyapati Thakur.
20567
The Brahmin category 'Shrotriya' was distinguished from others 
during the reign of which later Maharaja?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
20568
What are the three main social categories of Maithil Brahmins 
derived from the Panji system?
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddha (or Jaibar).
20569
Which script is traditionally used to write the Panji records in 
Mithila?
Tirhuta (or Mithilakshar).
20570
What does the term 'Chhadwith' refer to in Panji terminology?
The sixteen specific prohibited relationship points/ancestors that
