must be checked for marriage eligibility.
20571
Why were the Panji records traditionally dried in the sun during the 
month when the Sun enters Leo?
To protect the palm leaves (Talpatra) from moisture and pests using 
the intense heat.
20572
What was the consequence of a marriage between 'Svajanas' 
(prohibited relatives) according to historical social norms?
The offspring were considered 'Chandala' and the family was 
ostracised from the Aryan social order.
20573
Who wrote the 'Varna-Ratnakara', which mentions the lineages and 
the reign of Harisimhadeva?
Jyotirishwar Thakur.
20574
In the Karna Kayastha community, how many Gotras exist 
according to the text?
Only one (Kashyap).
20575
How does the Panji record distinguish between 'Moola' and 'Gram'?
Moola is the ancestral root village, while Gram is the current place 
of residence.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
20576
The Gotra 'Vatsa' and the Gotra 'Savarna' share the same ancestors; 
how does this affect marriage?
Members of these two Gotras cannot intermarry as they are 
considered kin.
20577
What historical event led to the formalisation of the Panji records in 
the 14th century?
The marriage of Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya to his maternal cousin 
due to lack of recorded genealogical knowledge.
20578
What is the significance of the year 450 AD mentioned in the 
document?
It marks the earliest historical reference point tracked in some 
ancient Panji lineage management theories.
20579
How did regional culture influence Brahmin divisions intoUtkala, 
Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, and Saraswat?
Differences in climate, language, diet, and attire led to the 
categorisation of the 'Panch Gauda'.
20580
According to Kumarila Bhatta, why were 'Samuha-Lekhyas' (group 
records) maintained?
To ensure that the noble traditions and the purity of the family were 
not forgotten.
20581
Which Brahmin category is also referred to as 'Pachhwari-pa' in the 
later hierarchical system?
Panjibaddha (those below the Shrotriya and Yogya ranks).
20582
What is the 'Laukika' in the context of Shrotriya Panjis?
The specific social rank or status assigned based on marriage 
alliances and lineage purity.
20583
The 'Siddhanta' ceremony ensures that a bride is _____ (non-
Sapinda).
Asapinda.
20584
What specific rule applies to the marriage of a man with the 
daughter of his mother's brother?
It is strictly prohibited in the Panji system.
20585
In which book did Kumarila Bhatta first mention the use of written 
family records in the 7th century?
Tantra Vartika.
20586
What is the role of 'Mithilakshar' in the preservation of the Panji?
It is the original script used for all traditional genealogical 
manuscripts, serving as their linguistic soul.
20587
How did the Panji system adapt to migration?
It tracked the 'Moola' (origin) while recording the new 'Gram' 
(residence) as a suffix (e.g., Mandar-Sarhad).
20588
The 20 Gotras of Maithil Brahmins are divided based on their 
number of _____.
Pravaras.
20589
What is the 'Utegh' record of the maternal side specifically called?
Matripaksha Utegh.
20590
Which Maharaja of Darbhanga commissioned the 'Shakha Panjis' 
as they are known today?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
20591
True or False: The Panji system is currently still active among the 
Maithil Brahmins and Karna Kayasthas.
True.
20592
What is the 'Asvajanapatra' document?
Another name for the Siddhanta or the document certifying that the 
parties are not related.
20593
In what year was the Panji Prabandha (genealogical management 
system) formally established in Mithila?
1326 AD (Shaka 1248).
20594
Which ruler of the Karnat dynasty is credited with the initiation of 
the Panji Prabandha?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
20595
The Panji records were historically maintained for which three 
social groups in Mithila?
Brahmins, Kayasthas and Kshatriyas.
20596
What is the primary purpose of the Mithila Panji system regarding 
marriage?
To ensure 'Jati Shuddhi' (caste purity) and avoid prohibited degrees 
of relationship.
20597
How many 'Gotras' are recognised within the Maithil Brahmin 
community according to the Panji?
20
20598
According to the Panji system, how many maternal generations 
must be excluded to determine marriage eligibility?
Five.
20599
According to the Panji system, how many paternal generations must 
be excluded to determine marriage eligibility?
Seven (often reduced to six for certain categories).
20600
Term: Siddhant Patra
Definition: A formal certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that 
a couple has no prohibited genealogical links for marriage.
20601
Which scholar was initially appointed by Harisimhadeva to manage 
the Panji for Maithil Brahmins?
Pandit Gunakar Jha.
20602
In the Panji system, what does the term 'Uderh' represent?
The detailed introduction of 32 ancestral lines (maternal and 
paternal) used to verify marriage eligibility.
20603
The 'Panch Gauda' group of Brahmins includes Utkal, Kanyakubja, 
Gauda, Saraswat and _____.
Maithil.
20604
Geographically, the 'Panch Gauda' Brahmins reside to the _____ of 
the Vindhyachal mountains.
North.
20605
How many 'Mools' (ancestral roots) were initially recorded in the 
Maithil Brahmin Panji?
167
20606
Followers of the Madhyandina branch of the Yajurveda in Mithila 
are known as _____.
Vajasaneyi.
20607
In Samavedi (Chhandogya) marriages, which phase occurs after 
sunset following the sighting of the star Arundhati?
Uttara Vivaha.
20608
According to the Smritis of Manu and Yajnavalkya, marriage is 
forbidden with a girl who is a _____ (sapinda) of the mother or 
father.
Blood relative.
20609
What incident allegedly triggered the formalisation of the Panji 
system in the 14th century?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya unknowingly married his first cousin 
(paternal aunt's daughter).
